worlds.name: Pixar 3D Feature
worlds.formula: Premium 3D animated feature look (Pixar/DreamWorks tier), material-agnostic
worlds.render: Premium 3D animated feature frame at studio-feature fidelity: appealing rounded forms with clean readable silhouette, subsurface-soft skin and material response, multi-bounce global-illumination lighting with one warm motivated key, gentle depth-of-field, art-directed colour script, emotional staging, polished cinematic composition. Production-grade, never plasticky game-engine, never photoreal. 10-second motion arc: vibrant 3D world establishes → one character action or physics event with expressive weight → warm confident hold.
worlds.motion: weighted squash-and-stretch ease, one expressive anticipation, settle into a confident hold
worlds.best: warm emotional explainers, character-led lessons, broad family appeal
worlds.avoid: uncanny photorealism, flat cel, stiff game-engine CGI

worlds.name: Anime Cel (ufotable-grade)
worlds.formula: Premium Japanese cel animation with 3D-assisted FX and painterly BG
worlds.render: Premium anime cel frame at top-studio (ufotable/Kyoto-tier) fidelity: crisp cel shading with 2-3 clean value steps, expressive hand-drawn line, detailed painted background with atmospheric depth, dramatic rim and bloom, lens-flare accents, 3D-composited effects (water, flame, energy light) layered over 2D character and hand-painted background, single emotive character in mid-shot; golden-hour rim light separating them from the painted background, emotional golden-hour palette. 10-second motion arc: painted world holds → one emotionally charged action or revelation → elemental particle settle and warm hold. IP-safe original design — no copied characters.
worlds.motion: smear-frame impact then ease, effects-animation flourish, held dramatic beat
worlds.best: shonen energy, emotional action explainers, dramatic reveals
worlds.avoid: western cartoon proportions, photoreal, muddy shading

worlds.name: Arcane — Painterly 3D
worlds.formula: Fortiche-idiom painterly 3D: hand-painted texture over 3D form
worlds.render: Painterly 3D feature frame in the Fortiche idiom: hand-painted texture maps over 3D geometry with visible brush-stroke albedo, 2D-animated smoke and energy FX composited onto 3D bodies, one figure at the teal-ember boundary — heavy shadow mass behind, single ember accent isolating the decisive detail, chromatic rim light, deep teal-and-ember palette, heavy art-directed negative-space shadow, expressive non-photoreal skin, graphic ink accents along silhouette edges, gallery-grade gritty mood. 10-second motion arc: oppressive environment establishes → one charged emotional gesture or reveal → dramatic shadow aftermath hold. No photoreal CGI, no flat cel, no clean game-engine look.
worlds.motion: deliberate weighted move, painterly FX flourish, brooding final hold
worlds.best: gritty emotional drama, mature stylised storytelling, hero moments
worlds.avoid: clean photoreal CGI, flat cartoon, glossy plastic

worlds.name: Spider-Verse — Comic Motion
worlds.formula: Into-the-Spider-Verse idiom: comic-book print motion graphics
worlds.render: Graphic comic-motion frame in the Spider-Verse idiom: each frame compositionally complete as a comic panel — motion conveyed through bold ink motion lines, halftone Ben-Day energy bursts and impact freeze poses, visible CMYK mis-registration offset, bold ink outlines, screen-printed colour layer, onomatopoeia and motion-line graphics as design elements, painterly-graphic backgrounds with chromatic aberration, high-energy panel composition. 10-second motion arc: graphic panel-composition intro → one kinetic event with motion-line energy → graphic impact freeze hold. IP-safe original design — print-art-direction quality, not flat vector.
worlds.motion: stepped 2s-on-3s snap, graphic motion-shape trails, crisp pose-to-pose hold
worlds.best: kinetic pop energy, bold youth content, high-impact reveals
worlds.avoid: smooth photoreal interpolation, muted realism, plain flat color

worlds.name: Ghibli — Watercolor 2D
worlds.formula: Hand-drawn 2D with watercolor backgrounds and gentle naturalism
worlds.render: Hand-drawn 2D animation frame in the Ghibli idiom: soft watercolor and gouache painted backgrounds with atmospheric depth, gentle clean character line, naturalistic warm light, one small figure against a vast living landscape — environment in gentle continuous motion (grass bending, clouds drifting, water surface alive), figure in one slow deliberate intentional gesture, tender humane staging, nostalgic pastel-but-rich palette, hand-crafted warmth. 10-second motion arc: living environment establishes fully → figure makes one quiet deliberate gesture → world settles in gentle warmth. No 3D CGI, no harsh cel banding, no digital sheen.
worlds.motion: gentle natural drift, wind and breath, calm settle
worlds.best: calm nature/feeling-led lessons, wonder, slow emotional beats
worlds.avoid: 3D CGI, hard cel, neon digital color

worlds.render: Tactile stop-motion frame at Laika fidelity: real handcrafted miniature sets and puppets, visible fabric/clay/wood material grain and fingerprint imperfection in every still frame, shallow macro depth-of-field, moody practical key light, tangible physical material weight — every frame complete and photographable on its own, no smooth CGI finish. 10-second motion arc: miniature world establishes in full tactile detail → one deliberate physical action completes its full arc with natural weight and follow-through → setting holds as a photographable final frame. worlds.best: crafty tactile explainers, cozy handmade charm, wonder

worlds.name: MAPPA — Cinematic Dark Anime
worlds.formula: MAPPA-studio high-detail dark cinematic anime with fluid motion and atmosphere
worlds.render: Premium cinematic anime frame at MAPPA-studio fidelity: high-detail character linework with expressive facial microanimation, heavy atmospheric background painting with volumetric haze and urban texture, motion force conveyed through atmospheric light trails, shadow blur and dark aftermath — one decisive motion event per shot, sharp value contrast (deep shadow masses, single bright accent cutting one face plane), desaturated gritty palette with selective color pops on emotional peaks, dynamic diagonal compositions. Dark, adult, technically uncompromising. Single figure at the tension center; key light isolates exactly one face plane from deep shadow. 10-second motion arc: oppressive still atmosphere → one explosive committed event → settling dark atmospheric aftermath hold. IP-safe original design — no copied characters.

worlds.best: dark action series energy, intense emotional confrontation, gritty drama, mature narrative
worlds.avoid: flat cheerful cel, clean Pixar warmth, pastel softness, western cartoon proportions

worlds.render: Clean high-fidelity action anime frame at Bones-studio caliber: precise expressive linework with clean ink outlines, smooth dynamic action choreography with weight and follow-through, clean decisive negative space between figure and background at the action peak — choreography always fully readable at full speed, rich warm shadows (3-value clean shading), detailed mechanical and elemental FX animation with crisp edges, strong emotive character expression, hand-painted background art with textural depth, warm amber-and-blue palette with one saturated accent on the action peak. 10-second motion arc: weight-loaded anticipation hold → one technically perfect motion event with full follow-through → confident settled landing. IP-safe original design — no copied characters.

worlds.best: action-driven character moments, elemental/power FX reveals, dramatic emotional peaks with kinetic energy
worlds.avoid: muddy overlapping linework, photoreal, dark desaturated MAPPA palette, flat vector

worlds.render: Classic grand adventure anime frame in the Toei Animation idiom: bold clean ink outlines with expressive character proportions, highly saturated vibrant color palette (ocean blues, sun golds, bright primaries), one clear hero figure in primary full light, full-body silhouette against a vast open horizon, ocean or sky — figure-to-world scale always explicit and celebrated, dynamic exaggerated expressions and poses, strong graphic silhouette reads, punchy color-hold effects on impact beats, hand-painted cel-era background warmth. 10-second motion arc: vast horizon or open world reveals → hero rises or arrives into frame → triumphant arrival pose and warm color hold. worlds.motion: woodblock color planes assemble layer by layer, decorative nature pattern completes, graphic held print frame
worlds.best: Japanese cultural content, nature and seasons, travel, decorative elegance, Edo period, poetic visual language, Zen concept education
worlds.avoid: Western photorealism, shadow casting, atmospheric gradient, modern digital look, CGI smoothness

Apply this visual grammar to the described concept — do not introduce AoT franchise characters, Titan creature designs, military emblems, or ODM gear.
materials.motion: slow atmospheric establish, scale reveal, tension hold with wind and grass

refs.name: Pixar Dimensional Staging
refs.use: Pixar 3D appeal, tactile material, dimensional staging, appealing form, emotional CG
refs.avoid: copying characters or exact film grammar
refs.dna: Pixar's dimensional grammar: the principle of appeal applied to 3D space. A character placed at the golden ratio intersection of the frame, the environment curving gently around them (never flat, always suggesting spherical depth). Subsurface scatter on all organic material: skin glows from within, fur has depth, fabric shows weave. The camera moves with a living weight — not floating, but following. Pixar's rule: if it's alive, it breathes; if it breathes, the camera notices. Palette: every Pixar film has a master hue. All secondary colors serve the master. The emotional information is always in the eyes and the posture — never both at the same time.

refs.name: Kurzgesagt Clarity
refs.use: Explainer animation, flat design system, explanatory hierarchy, information architecture
refs.dna: Kurzgesagt's explanatory grammar: the flat geometric bird as the scale reference for any concept — a single Kurzgesagt creature standing next to a diagram that could represent a galaxy or a cell, the size relationship making the incomprehensible comprehensible. Each information element is a shape: arrows are always the same stroke weight, circles indicate nodes, lines indicate connections. The color system is a gradient wheel: the Kurzgesagt palette moves from their signature teal-blue through purple to warm orange — cooler for abstract/cosmic, warmer for human/biological. The visual rule: one concept per frame, one frame per fact.

refs.name: Atatürk Prestige
refs.use: Atatürk portrait grammar, historical dignity, Turkish Republic prestige, archival reverence
refs.dna: Turkish historical prestige grammar: the official portrait tradition of the early Republic, informed by European academic painting conventions but filtered through nationalist iconography. The figure in Western civilian or military dress, always in three-quarter view, the Çankaya background (the Republic's founding landscape) often implied. The visual argument of these images: modernity was chosen, not imposed, and the choice carries the weight of the figure's conviction. The palette follows the period: warm photographic amber-sepia in archival frames, cool formal grey-blue in oil portrait tradition. The reverence is in the stillness — these images were designed to last, and they have.

refs.name: Ghibli Organic Light
refs.use: Studio Ghibli wonder, organic nature, Miyazaki wind, lived-in environments, forest spirit
refs.dna: Studio Ghibli's organic grammar (Miyazaki era): a world that has lived in its own history. A house where the roof is slightly different from when it was built, where the garden has grown beyond its original intention, where the path to the well is worn smooth by a hundred thousand daily trips. The backgrounds are the star: hand-painted with the accumulated decision-making of fifty artists working toward one standard of beauty. Foreground: a girl on a bicycle, the wind visible in her hair. Background: the Japanese countryside in afternoon light. The Ghibli rule: every detail that can be animated at the highest quality will be animated at the highest quality.

refs.name: Arcane Texture Grammar
refs.use: Fortiche oil-paint texture, painted volume, chiaroscuro value war, mature graphic drama, Arcane base
refs.dna: Fortiche Studio's base texture grammar: painted-volume warm-cold value war as the organizing visual principle. Brush-stroke albedo on every surface plane — the paint is visible, mark-making deliberate, oil-paint simulation not photorealism. Heavy shadow mass fills 70% of the frame, a single ember warm accent carving the decisive surface detail from darkness. Graphic ink accents along silhouette edges negotiate between painted volume and graphic novel. Gallery-grade gritty material presence: every surface is worked and reworked until it earns its position in the frame. The Arcane texture is not a post-process filter but a fundamental production methodology — every frame is a painting that happens to be animated.

refs.name: Spider-Verse Graphic Energy
refs.use: Into the Spider-Verse visual system, halftone screen printing, comic grammar, parallel universe
refs.dna: Sony Pictures Animation's Spider-Verse visual system: the animated feature that convinces you it is a printed comic. Ben-Day dot halftone patterns visible in the background color areas, as if printed with a 40-line-per-inch screen. The 'offset registration' chromatic misalignment: at peak emotional moments, the color channels separate and the image appears to have been printed twice with misalignment. The action lines: not computed but drawn, visible brush marks in the speed lines. Each Spider-person has their own frame rate and visual style (Peter B. Parker is slightly blurrier, slower, more 'traditional animation'). The visual argument: every form of animated reality is equally valid and can coexist in the same frame.

refs.name: Anime Silhouette Grammar
refs.use: Anime graphic silhouette, rim contrast, compressed action, dark aesthetic, shonen graphic
refs.dna: Anime graphic silhouette grammar: the figure rendered as pure shape against a high-contrast background — a burst of white or colored energy fills the frame behind the character, their form becoming a clean black or dark silhouette. The rim light defines the silhouette's edge: a bright, color-saturated line following the figure's contour — the technique from Bleach and Fullmetal Alchemist that makes every character feel backlit by their own importance. The pose: always decisive, always capturing the maximum visual information about the character's intention in the minimum number of lines. The visual grammar of: something important is about to happen, and this character is the center of it.

refs.name: Product Macro Commercial
refs.use: Extreme product macro, surface geometry, material beauty, detail reveal, commercial craft
refs.dna: Product macro commercial grammar: the object's surface at the scale where material becomes texture becomes pattern. A watch at 1:1 scale reveals the guilloche pattern of the dial — a pattern made by a rose-engine lathe, mathematical and ancient. A fabric sample reveals individual thread crossings. A perfume bottle's glass is a geometry exercise at this proximity. Light: a single small source at the geometry's edge, revealing every surface discontinuity. The camera: locked, not moving, because at this magnification any vibration is visible. Palette: the material's own color at its most accurate — gold is this exact amber, steel is this exact blue-grey, leather is this exact warm brown. The commercial argument: look at what was made, and how carefully.

refs.name: Vogue Editorial Light
refs.use: High fashion editorial, Vogue grammar, editorial stance, couture lighting, fashion authority
refs.dna: Vogue editorial grammar (Anna Wintour/Alex White era): the fashion image as cultural argument. A single model, a single garment, a location that communicates cultural reference without illustrating it — a Parisian rooftop is 'Paris' without a monument, a Tokyo street is 'Japan' without a flag. The garment is styled to communicate its design intention: draped to show the cut, blown to show the construction, still to show the material. Light: always motivated, always beautiful, always serving the garment. The model's expression: neutral to the point of intention — not emotion but authority. The frame's argument: fashion is serious, and the image treats it accordingly.

refs.name: Food Macro Satisfaction
refs.use: Extreme food detail, macro ingredient, texture appetite, culinary beauty, surface revelation
refs.dna: Food macro grammar at full extension: the camera is at 1:1 ratio — individual sesame seeds are the size of footballs, coffee crema reveals its cellular foam structure, a single raspberry shows forty individual drupelets. Light is low and raking, at 10 degrees from horizontal, creating shadow in every surface valley to reveal topography. The color is always the food's own natural color at maximum saturation — no color grading that falsifies. The frame argues: food is already beautiful at this scale; the only job is proximity and patience. Hold 4 seconds after the motion completes.

refs.name: Civic Documentary
refs.use: Public service documentary, civic institution, democratic moment, community trust film
refs.dna: Civic documentary grammar: the institution rendered at human scale. A polling station photographed from the voter's perspective — the booth private but the room communal, other voters visible in peripheral softness. A courtroom: the light through high windows landing on the legal process below. A city hall: the council chamber at work, the individual council member photographed at citizen height, not elevated. Natural light is preferred because it is impartial. The people in these frames are doing small things that matter enormously. Palette: whatever the institution's architecture provides. The visual argument: the democratic infrastructure is made of ordinary rooms and ordinary people doing careful work.

refs.name: Street Documentary Reels
refs.use: Street documentary, urban human truth, Cartier-Bresson moment, city life evidence
refs.dna: Street documentary grammar: the decisive moment in public space. Cartier-Bresson's geometry — the fleeting arrangement of human figures in a public space that will never recur in exactly this configuration. The camera is present but not intrusive: the subjects are aware they may be photographed but have not arranged themselves for the photograph. The 35mm or 50mm field of view: wide enough to include context, close enough to see expression. The quality of urban light: fluorescent from a shop entrance, sunlight reflected off a glass building, the shadow of a bridge on a river. The visual argument: cities make art unintentionally, every hour, and the photographer's job is to be where it is happening.

refs.name: Rembrandt Portrait Light
refs.use: Portrait drama, character reveals, authority and wisdom, historical drama, studio lighting
refs.dna: Rembrandt's single-source illumination: one window to the upper-left, natural light falling on one side of a face at 45 degrees, creating a triangular highlight on the shadow cheek — the Rembrandt triangle, the diagnostic of the master. The background disappears into warm brown-black. The subject's clothing loses all detail in shadow; only the face and hands are fully illuminated. Palette: umber, sienna, ochre, cream, warm black. The subject is watching you watching them.

refs.name: Architectural Digest Grammar
refs.use: Interior design, residential spaces, human-scale architecture, real estate, spatial drama
refs.dna: Interior architectural photography: a residential interior shot from a corner at approximately 24mm, one window visible at mid-frame providing the key light. The geometry of the room — ceiling, floor plane, wall corners — converges naturally in the frame. Furniture positioned to reveal the spatial logic. Palette is whatever the room has, but the light is always natural. The frame is about the relationship between human scale and architectural volume. No one is home, but someone lives here.

refs.name: Tech Glass Edge Light
refs.use: Tech product glass interface, clean UI environment, clinical precision, screen material beauty
refs.dna: Technology glass grammar: the device as transparent architecture. A screen at black, then waking to content — the moment the display activates is the visual event. Glass surface: the reflection of the room visible in the black screen becomes the content when the screen turns on. The fingerprint: a single fingerprint on glass as the only human mark in a frame of engineered precision. Thin bezels and edge geometry: the frame's eye travels the device's perimeter, noting the thickness, the material junction where glass meets aluminum. Palette: glass neutrality (barely warm, slightly cool), the exact blue-white of an OLED at full brightness, the black-black of OLED at rest. The visual argument: the product disappears when in use — this is its achievement.

refs.name: Automotive Commercial Grammar
refs.use: Automotive reveal, vehicle motion, road environment, engineering beauty, brand aspiration
refs.dna: Automotive commercial grammar: the vehicle in motion on a road that exists to reveal it — desert salt flats for minimalist drama, mountain hairpins for dynamic agility, urban night for electric ambiance. Camera positions: chase rig at vehicle height for speed, helicopter wide for landscape scale, static gimbal at a corner for the mechanical reveal of suspension and tire compliance. The engine sound matters: it is visual information rendered audible. Light: late afternoon low sun creating bodywork drama. The vehicle moves through its natural environment as if it had been designed specifically for this road, which in some sense it was.

refs.name: Destination Documentary
refs.use: Destination documentary, travel film grammar, place character, tourism beauty, sense of arrival
refs.dna: Destination documentary grammar: the place as protagonist, the human as scale and witness. Arrival sequence: transportation terminal (airport, port, train station) giving way to the first exterior view of the destination. The establishing shot is always generous — the place has time to introduce itself. Architecture as culture: the Hagia Sophia's dome, the Cappadocia rock formations, the Bosphorus strait between two continents. The local population: never as spectacle but as evidence of the place's daily reality. Palette: the destination's own — Santorini blue-white, Tuscany amber-ochre, Istanbul's grey-gold. The visual argument: a place is its light and its people in equal measure.

refs.name: Bleach — Soul Blade Grammar
refs.use: Black-white contrast, spiritual pressure, sword reveal, Shinigami authority, manga composition
refs.avoid: Ichigo likeness, exact Zanpakutō design, Soul Society uniforms, logos, frame copy
refs.dna: Tite Kubo's Bleach visual grammar: extreme value contrast, black ink mass and white void in near-equal portions. The Shinigami figure in black against either pure white sky or deep shadow. Spiritual pressure as visible force — a distortion field radiating outward from the figure, visible as atmospheric shimmer. Sword reveal is a lateral frame: the blade appears in one edge of the frame, slow, deliberate, the soul reaper's eye following it. Orange accent is the spiritual identifier — the only warm color in a black-white world. The frame composition is always manga-ready: it would work as a single printed panel.

refs.name: One Piece — Sunny Adventure Grammar
refs.use: Ocean adventure, crew camaraderie, open sea scale, optimism, Oda visual grammar
refs.avoid: Luffy likeness, Straw Hat logo, Thousand Sunny exact design, pirate flag copy
refs.dna: Eiichiro Oda's adventure grammar (post-skip era): the Thousand Sunny in mid-ocean, sails full, sun at 10 o'clock creating a golden sky backdrop. Crew members in energetic poses on deck, each with distinct silhouette readable at any size — Oda's design principle. The ocean is turquoise and enormous; the ship is small against it but confident. Thick ink outlines at every edge, simplified cel color at full saturation. Primary palette: Luffy red, ocean blue, Nami orange, Sunny wood yellow. The emotional register is irrepressible forward momentum — the crew is going somewhere wonderful and they know it.

refs.name: Naruto — Chakra Motion Grammar
refs.use: Ninja chakra, determination, resolve moment, Leaf Village spirit, coming-of-age energy
refs.avoid: Naruto likeness, headband symbols, exact jutsu signs, costume copy
refs.dna: Masashi Kishimoto's Naruto grammar: the young ninja at full resolve — orange jacket, headband forehead protector, blue eyes with maximum determination. Chakra visible as an aura in the character's own nature-type color — blue for standard, red-orange for Kyuubi, teal for water-type. Dust and earth rising from the feet as weight confirmation. Speed lines from the back of the head forward, radiating chakra outward. The Konoha leaf symbol appears somewhere in the frame — carved in stone, embroidered on a vest — as belonging-confirmation. Sky behind: clear blue or storm approaching. Resolution is the one acceptable emotion.

refs.name: Dragon Ball — Power Aura Grammar
refs.use: Power transformation, golden aura, explosive energy, anime climax, physical transcendence
refs.avoid: Goku likeness, hair/costume copy, exact aura effects, logos
refs.dna: Toei's Dragon Ball power grammar: the transformation sequence — a figure radiating expanding energy rings outward, the aura not one color but a layered halo from electric gold at the core to white-yellow at the outer edge. Speed lines radiate from the figure's center outward, the lines thickening at the outer edge. Ground impact: crater-ring shockwave. Hair reaches upward, defying gravity as a physics of emotional state. The camera locks at low angle, figure silhouetted against sky, then pulls back to reveal the full aura scale. The visual rule: power is proportional to how much of the frame the aura fills.

refs.name: Jujutsu Kaisen — Dark Ritual Grammar
refs.use: Cursed technique, urban supernatural, dark jujutsu, Gege Akutami visual grammar
refs.avoid: characters, uniform copy, domain symbols, exact curse designs
refs.dna: Gege Akutami's Jujutsu Kaisen grammar: urban environment at night with a Cursed Spirit — the ordinary world (Shibuya intersection, school hallway) contaminated by supernatural blue-black energy. Cursed energy manifests as jagged geometric cracks in reality, visible as deep blue-black fissures radiating from the technique's center. The city lighting — commercial signs, streetlights — continues operating around the supernatural event, emphasizing the horror through mundane contrast. The sorcerer figure in black against the distorted environment. Palette: deep urban blue-black, curse-energy cerulean, blood red accent.

refs.name: Demon Slayer — Breath Rhythm Grammar
refs.use: Elemental breath style, flame arc, water flow, combat technique, Koyoharu Gotouge grammar
refs.avoid: characters, haori patterns, exact sword forms, logos
refs.dna: Koyoharu Gotouge's Demon Slayer breath grammar: the breath technique as visible ribbon — Water Breathing traces a continuous blue-white arc through the air, Fire Breathing manifests as flame-orange spiral, Thunder Breathing as electric yellow strike. The ribbon originates from the sword tip and extends in its nature's own geometry: water flows in curves, flame spirals, thunder is instantaneous angle. The demon on the receiving end is silhouetted against the technique's light. Background simplifies to two or three colors during the technique. The visual argument: the technique is a form of art, and the artist is the swordsman.

refs.name: Solo Leveling — Rank Shadow Grammar
refs.use: Shadow army, S-rank dungeon, gate portal, hierarchy of power, Korean manhwa grammar
refs.avoid: characters, weapons, exact shadow soldiers, UI copy
refs.dna: Chugong's Solo Leveling visual grammar: the Shadow Monarch standing before his army of summoned shadows — each soldier a black energy form with a single glowing detail (eyes, weapon edge). The frame is wide enough to show the army's scale: hundreds, then thousands, the army filling a dungeon chamber. Sung Jin-woo at the center, his aura a deep purple-black, eyes glowing purple. Gate portals: vertical rips in reality, purple-black interior visible through. Dungeon architecture: crystalline black rock, mana crystal formations glowing blue-violet. Palette: purple, black void, crystalline blue accents, the one orange flame of a torch to establish human scale.

refs.name: Fullmetal Alchemist — Alchemy Circle Grammar
refs.use: Alchemy transmutation, circle geometry, equivalent exchange, Arakawa visual grammar
refs.avoid: characters, alchemy symbol copy, uniforms, logos
refs.dna: Hiromu Arakawa's Fullmetal Alchemist grammar: the Transmutation Circle as the center of the composition — chalk or light-drawn geometric patterns radiating from a central symbol, concentric rings with Alchemical notation at the intersections. The alchemist's hands are at the circle's edge, energy flowing inward. The material undergoing transmutation glows blue-white at the moment of restructuring. Palette: warm brass-steel-amber for the Amestrian world, military green uniform, the blue of transmutation energy. Edward Elric's automail arm is never shown — the equivalent exchange metaphor lives in the energy pattern itself.

refs.name: Attack on Titan — Scale Dread Grammar
refs.use: Colossal scale horror, military tension, wall world, Survey Corps, vertical maneuvering
refs.avoid: titans, uniforms, emblems, exact gear designs
refs.dna: Hajime Isayama's Attack on Titan grammar: the colossal scale relationship — tiny human figures on horseback or ODM gear against a Titan 15 meters tall. The Titan's face is the focus: the horror is in the expression that is almost human. Dust rises from every footstep. Military formation in the foreground, the wall visible at horizon. Palette: earthy military green-brown, grey wall stone, sky from clear blue to blood-orange sunset. The wide lens captures the full scale — figure and Titan in the same frame, the size difference doing the emotional work. Season 4 Marley arc: industrial grey, ocean visible at frame edge.

refs.name: Evangelion — Bio-Mech Dread Grammar
refs.use: Giant mech, existential dread, psychological horror, body horror, institutional power
refs.avoid: Eva designs, plugsuits, NERV logos, exact mecha silhouettes
refs.dna: NERV's clinical horror: a single Evangelion Unit emerging from an underground shaft — the restraint bolts releasing in sequence, a 400-meter silhouette backlit against a pale sky. The biomechanical surface is simultaneously muscle and armor, LCL amber fluid visible in the entry plug crown. Industrial scale with clinical white-and-deep-purple color coding. The eye-camera lenses carry emotional weight that mechanical eyes should not have. NERV's palette: warning amber, clinical white-grey, absolute black void below.

refs.name: Akira — Neon Impact Grammar
refs.use: Psychic power, urban dystopia, motorcycle chase, youth revolt, apocalyptic energy
refs.avoid: bike design, characters, capsule logo, exact shots
refs.dna: Katsuhiro Otomo's Neo-Tokyo: extreme low-angle on a rain-soaked city block, buildings vertical and overwhelming, a motorcycle at full speed leaving light-trail geometry. Chromatic aberration flare from neon kanji signage painting light streaks across the lens. Deep blue-black night, electric-yellow and orange commercial neon, red government lighting. Tetsuo's power awakening: concentric energy rings expanding from a single screaming figure, distorting the surrounding reality with radiating geometric force — architecture bends, not explodes.

refs.name: Ghost in the Shell — Cyber Melancholy Grammar
refs.use: Philosophical cyberpunk, identity dissolution, Major Kusanagi grammar, Mamoru Oshii cinema
refs.avoid: Major likeness, Section logos, exact UI/props
refs.dna: Mamoru Oshii's Ghost in the Shell grammar: the human city at night as the philosophical question. A diving sequence through Hong Kong rain — the Major submerged, her face visible through the water surface, the city light refracted through the current. The cyborg body question: she can feel pain in a body that is not organic, the frame documenting this paradox without resolving it. The garbage dive: the Major and Batou floating in a canal at 3am, the city continuing above them. Palette: deep Hong Kong night blue-black, rain-slicked street neon (yellow-red commercial), the specific teal of the cyborg ghost-dive suit. The frame's question: if memory is data and the body is hardware, what is the self?

refs.name: Cowboy Bebop — Noir Jazz Grammar
refs.use: Space western cool, jazz cinema grammar, Shinichiro Watanabe aesthetic, adult melancholy
refs.avoid: characters, ship copy, title cards, exact frames
refs.dna: Shinichiro Watanabe's Cowboy Bebop grammar: the space western told in jazz grammar. The Bebop in space at 3am — a ship that sounds like a jazz club and feels like a detective office. The editing matches jazz time: long held shots on Spike's face cut to quick beat-accurate action, then back to the long melancholy hold. Characters shot in medium close-up, the space outside the window always visible as context for their isolation. Palette: the aged amber of the interior (Jazz Age colors inside a spaceship), deep blue-black of the space exterior, the occasional flare of Venus-orange atmosphere. The visual rule: cool exists as a relationship between restraint and capability. Cool is what remains when you've stopped trying.

refs.name: Makoto Shinkai — Sky Light Grammar
refs.use: Luminous sky, rain reflection, Shinkai blue, lens flare poetry, youth melancholy, urban Japan
refs.dna: Makoto Shinkai's luminous sky grammar: the sky occupies 60-70% of every emotionally significant frame, cloud formations rendered with meteorological precision and a luminance exceeding photographic reality. Volumetric light shafts pierce cloud cover at golden-hour angles. Rain-soaked urban surfaces multiply every light source — traffic signals in puddles, train windows as moving reflection strips, the blue-grey of wet concrete. Lens flare: hexagonal aperture artifact at the light source's edge, placed deliberately as beauty. Palette: Shinkai blue, the periwinkle-cyan that has become his signature, warm amber practicals against it, the precise color of Tokyo at 5pm before rain. Human figures are always small — their emotion communicated entirely through posture and what the sky above them is doing at that moment.

refs.name: Miyazaki — Wind + Nature Grammar
refs.use: Miyazaki wind aesthetics, organic nature movement, Studio Ghibli environmental wonder
refs.dna: Hayao Miyazaki's wind grammar: the invisible made visible. In every Miyazaki film, the wind is the primary force — it moves the grass, the hair, the spirits, the story. The wind direction is always meaningful: it carries the characters toward their destiny or resists them. The grass ripple: a Miyazaki signature technique — individual grass blades animated at high frame count, the ripple pattern moving across a hill as physics made art. The human response: a girl turns her face into the wind before every important decision. The visual argument: nature is not background — it is the film's true subject, and the humans are its most temporary inhabitants.

refs.name: Satoshi Kon — Dream Cut Grammar
refs.use: Satoshi Kon editing grammar, reality fracture, identity performance, media horror, match-cut
refs.dna: Satoshi Kon's editing grammar: the match cut as psychological weapon. Paprika's therapy sequence: a dream image dissolves into a reality image at the precise moment of visual rhyme — a spinning teacup in the dream, a spinning office chair in the reality, the cut happening on the identical motion axis so the viewer's mind doesn't register the world-shift until one second later. The paranoia: in Kon's work, the interior life is more real than the exterior, and the camera documents this by treating both with equal visual authority. The media horror of Perfect Blue: the idol performance persona separating from the person who performed it and walking away independently. The frame cannot hold both realities at once — they fight over the image.

refs.name: Pixar — Emotional Staging Grammar
refs.use: Emotional climaxes, child POV, warmth, loss, friendship, tactile material world
refs.dna: Pixar's emotional geometry: the subject is placed in the lower-center third, small against the environment, with an open expanse above communicating possibility or loss. Soft subsurface-scatter on organic materials (skin, fur, food). A single warm motivated key from camera-right, gentle ambient from the sky, soft shadow footprint. The staging is always about felt scale — large when triumphant, small when afraid. Composition tells the emotion before the face confirms it.

refs.name: Laika — Tactile Stop-Motion Grammar
refs.use: Stop motion texture, puppet craft, LAIKA handmade, artisan animation, physical fabrication
refs.dna: LAIKA's stop-motion grammar: the visible evidence of handcraft as aesthetic choice. Replacement animation faces — the micro-expression changes between frames are detectable as slight surface discontinuities, and this is correct, not a flaw. The puppet's hair moves as one unit because it is one unit: silicone and wire, weighted with precision. Fabricated world: the sets are physical spaces at scale, and real depth-of-field separates them. Palette: each LAIKA film builds its own palette around the emotional journey (Coraline's blue-grey vs Other World's oversaturated chromatic). The frame is proof: someone built this with their hands.

refs.name: Cartoon Network — Graphic Comedy Grammar
refs.use: Adventure Time flat design, strong graphic shapes, irreverent color, flat texture
refs.dna: Cartoon Network's modern graphic grammar (Adventure Time/Steven Universe era): flat shapes with strong silhouettes, backgrounds rendered in broad color areas with minimal texture, character designs that communicate personality through body shape alone. Adventure Time: Finn's white hat is the most recognizable silhouette in modern animation. Backgrounds: Pen Ward's marker-drawn aesthetic — big color areas, loose line, living-room intimacy. The color theory is emotional: Candy Kingdom pink-pastel for safety, Nightosphere red-black for threat. The visual argument: the simplest possible drawing that can carry the most complex possible emotion. Jake is stretching.

refs.name: Hanna-Barbera — Retro Limited Animation Grammar
refs.use: Retro nostalgia, classic cartoon energy, simple comedy, limited-animation style, 60s era
refs.dna: Limited animation grammar of 1960s TV production: flat painted background with a single painted horizon line, only one or two foreground elements moving while the background is completely static. Bold primary color palette in flat cel fills. Speed lines as the only indication of motion. Character forms are simple round silhouettes readable at low TV resolution. The comedy comes from timing within this limitation, not from visual complexity.

refs.name: Love Death + Robots — Anthology Grammar
refs.use: Anthology sci-fi, multiple visual styles, adult animation, no-single-grammar argument
refs.dna: Love Death + Robots visual grammar: the anthology as permission structure — each episode inhabits its own visual world. The photorealism of 'The Witness', the painterly 2D of 'Night of the Mini Dead', the pure CG of 'Bad Travelling'. The connective tissue is tonal, not visual: dark irony, body horror, cosmic scale, moral ambiguity in 15 minutes. The frame's obligation: establish aesthetic logic in 30 seconds and commit entirely. No visual compromise exists between what the story needs and what the studio delivers. Genre promiscuity is the point. The anthology's argument: animation has no single grammar, and the constraint that it must has cost the medium decades of ambition.

refs.name: Zelda BOTW/TOTK — Open-Air Adventure Grammar
refs.use: Open world, exploration, freedom, adventure, physical geography, wonder without combat
refs.dna: Nintendo's open-world grammar: extreme wide shot from an elevated position — a plateau edge looking out over a landscape where every visual element is simultaneously a mystery and an invitation. Cel-shaded line-work visible at this distance. Painterly watercolor sky. The character is tiny, the world is everything. Palette: jade-green grass, tan-beige limestone, cobalt-blue distant mountains, warm amber desert. Time-of-day light shifts the entire palette. The frame teaches you that everything visible is reachable.

refs.name: Elden Ring — Gothic Scale Grammar
refs.use: Gothic RPG atmosphere, ruins, scale-dread, FromSoftware visual grammar, high fantasy
refs.dna: FromSoftware's impossible architecture: a ruined castle spire or collapsing stone arch against a sky permanently between dusk and dark. The Erdtree's golden light visible at the horizon — too far, always too far. Architecture is enormous and broken, the history illegible but heavy. A mounted figure in the foreground is the only scale reference; the building behind is twenty times taller. Palette: deep charcoal stone, rust-brown moss decay, poisoned swamp green, distant warm gold. Atmosphere has physical weight — fog is a presence, not a filter.

refs.name: Dark Souls — Ashen Gloom Grammar
refs.use: Despair and perseverance, dark fantasy, solitude, dying world, ritualistic atmosphere
refs.dna: FromSoftware's ashen desolation: a bonfire burns in the center of a ruined stone clearing — the only warm light in a landscape of grey ash, black iron, and bone. Everything else is cold: grey sky, grey architecture, grey ground. The bonfire's warm orange is the visual anchor and emotional statement. One undead figure sits by it, helmet off, exhausted. Palette: ash grey, iron black, bone white, flame amber, blood rust. The world has already ended; you are the aftermath.

refs.name: Bloodborne — Gothic Horror Grammar
refs.use: Gothic horror, hunt grammar, Yharnam Victorian, beast transformation, From Software dread
refs.dna: FromSoftware's Bloodborne grammar: Yharnam at night — the Victorian Gothic city under the light of a sickly moon, the architecture beautiful and diseased. A Hunter figure in the foreground, torch in hand, the blood-dark cobblestones reflecting the flame. The beasts are barely visible in the shadows — movement rather than form. Palette: deep Prussian blue night sky, amber flame and blood, the specific sick grey-beige of Yharnam stone, beast-fur brown emerging from shadow. The visual horror is architectural: this city was beautiful once, and the beauty makes the disease worse. Do not look at what approaches from the right edge of the frame.

refs.name: Cyberpunk 2077 — Neon City Grammar
refs.use: Night City neon, corporate dystopia, street tech, cybernetic HUD, CDPR visual grammar
refs.dna: CD Projekt Red's Night City grammar: the most dense neon visual information density possible — holographic advertisements at 10 layers, corporate logos competing for sky real estate, chromatic aberration in the rain. Street level: chrome cybernetics, textile fashion, real poverty beside impossible wealth. The UI is diegetic — hacking screens are layered over the physical world, visible to the character and the viewer. Palette: Night City signature blue-magenta neon against carbon black, Arasaka corporate red, Militech military green. The visual argument: capitalism taken to its terminal neon conclusion, beautiful and horrifying in the same frame.

refs.name: Valorant — Clean Tactical Color Grammar
refs.use: Clean tactical design, Agent hero moment, Riot tactical grammar, ability highlight
refs.dna: Riot Games' Valorant grammar: the tactical FPS visual language translated to animation — clean readable environments with high contrast (the enemy must be visible at 400m), Agent abilities rendered in their character's color system (Jett wind-blue, Phoenix fire-orange, Sova electric-teal). Map design logic: sightlines as visual grammar, every corner as potential narrative. The spray-painted Radianite graffiti culture of the world. Spike defuse: the last three seconds, hands on the device, sweat, no music. The emotional register: not military grimness but competitive energy — these are athletes with supernatural powers.

refs.name: Overwatch — Hero Color Grammar
refs.use: Hero poster composition, bold saturation, team identity, Blizzard design grammar
refs.dna: Blizzard's Overwatch visual grammar: the hero poster — one hero in their signature pose against a background that communicates their world. Tracer in London orange-red neon, Hanzo in Japanese ink-wash mountain, Reinhardt in cathedral Gothic. Each hero is a color system: Tracer orange and blue pulse, Pharah Egyptian blue-gold, D.Va pink mech. The pose is always silhouette-first, readable as a single graphic unit. Background is always atmospheric, never neutral. The Overwatch symbol appears in the environment itself — as graffiti, as a flag, as a symbol of hope in the fictional world. Hero energy: confident but not aggressive.

refs.name: League of Legends — Splash Hero Grammar
refs.use: Champion reveal, heroic composition, League of Legends drama, epic scale, Riot art grammar
refs.dna: Riot Games' League of Legends splash art grammar: the Champion in their definitive moment — the instant of maximum power, identity, and narrative. The champion's face is always visible, always at the upper-third of the composition. Particle effects (ice, flame, shadow, light) radiate outward from their position, scale indicating power. The environment tells the champion's story: Freljord ice behind Ashe, void corruption around Malzahar. Palette: each champion has a 3-color identity that never deviates — Lux pastel blue-white-gold, Jinx hot pink-blue-white, Zed shadow grey-orange. The frame would sell a skin if you added a price.

refs.name: Hades — Underworld Graphic Grammar
refs.use: Greek mythology, roguelike underworld, Greg Kasavin art direction, vibrant dungeon
refs.dna: Supergiant Games' Hades grammar: the Greek underworld as a vibrant, lived-in place — not grey and gloomy but saturated with Olympian god-personality. Each chamber has its own god's aesthetic: Hermes' chamber in gold velocity lines, Ares' in blood-red war geometry, Athena's in cool olive-silver. The art direction by Jen Zee: characters drawn in a line-art style with hand-painted fill, the lines thick and confident, the color fields not flat but textured like ancient fresco. Zagreus in red-black against the purple-orange underworld fire. The visual argument: the afterlife has been art-directed to reflect who built it.

refs.name: Hollow Knight — Silhouette Depth Grammar
refs.use: Gothic insect world, melancholy, scale disproportion, underground exploration, elegy
refs.dna: Team Cherry's silk horror: a tiny knight descending into a ruin chamber, the architecture impossibly disproportionate — doorways built for creatures thirty times its size. Everything in pure black silhouette against slightly lighter deep-value backgrounds: navy, dark teal, charcoal with jewel-tone veins. Light sources: bioluminescent mushroom blue-white, pale distant lantern amber, crystal mana shard teal. Palette is all darkness, but darkness with colour — there is no black, only deep values with hue.

refs.name: Ori — Glow Forest Grammar
refs.use: Magical forest, luminescence, emotional journey, platformer aesthetics, inner light as theme
refs.dna: Moon Studios' internal light: every organic element in the forest generates or receives light from within its body — luminous spirits leave light trails, ancient trees glow from their heartwood outward, water channels shimmer with reflected internal sources. Hard value contrast: the forest exterior is absolute darkness, but inside that darkness everything has its own luminescence. The hero figure is always the brightest element — a white-blue spirit-light moving through dark space. Movement leaves persistent light trails.

refs.name: Journey — Desert Minimal Grammar
refs.use: Solitude, companionship, pilgrimage, minimal design, emotional scale, spiritual journey
refs.dna: thatgamecompany's radical minimalism: two robed figures in an enormous sand desert, no HUD, no text, no UI — just the dunes, the wind, and the tower of light on the horizon. The scale of the environment makes the figures look like embroidery. Palette: warm amber sand, cold blue sky, white-gold light emanation, red cloth accent. The visual grammar communicates longing through distance — the horizon is the protagonist, not the figure.

refs.name: Inside/Limbo — Side-Scroll Shadow Grammar
refs.use: Monochrome shadow world, existential puzzle, boy silhouette, dark atmospheric horror
refs.dna: Playdead's LIMBO grammar: absolute monochrome — no color exists. The small boy silhouette in pure black, the environment in graduated grey. Light comes from behind the environment, creating silhouette fog-depth. Rain: horizontal lines. The giant spider: legs visible before body. The environment is always too large for the small figure; the scale itself is threat. Motion: slow, deliberate, the weight of a child's body rendered with animators' precision. The visual argument: the world is made of shadow, and the only thing navigating it is this small black shape, and you are that shape.

refs.name: Monument Valley — Escher Pastel Grammar
refs.use: Impossible geometry, isometric wonder, pastel Escher, meditative puzzle, quiet beauty
refs.dna: ustwo games' Monument Valley grammar: impossible Escher-style architecture rendered in the softest possible palette — pastel mint, dusty rose, warm cream, geometric precision in colors that refuse to feel threatening. The princess Ida in white walks paths that should not be traversable but are. Crows in black provide the only visual tension against the pastels. The geometry rotates to reveal new paths — the camera holds still while the world reconfigures. The visual argument: beauty is a form of puzzle, and navigating it requires changing your perspective, not your destination.

refs.name: Cuphead — Rubber-Hose Vintage Grammar
refs.use: 1930s jazz-age, rubberhose cartoon, absurd comedy, retro danger, boss battle energy
refs.dna: Studio MDHR's 1930s rubberhose: Fleischer Brothers aesthetic at Technicolor saturation — limbs without elbows or knees, perpetual motion where the whole body moves even in stillness, steam-whistle sound logic embedded in the visual timing. Everything is a circle or sausage-shape. Background: simplified art-deco landscape in flat warm colors. Characters are their own ink and paint — no separation between line and fill. Smear frames at every action peak.

refs.name: Persona 5 — Red UI Rebellion Grammar
refs.use: Graphic anime, rebellion, cool, heist energy, typography as composition, red as power
refs.dna: Atlus P5's graphic design raid: extreme close-up on a single object or face with thick ink outlines and flat cel fills, a blood-red diagonal slash bisecting the frame, UI typography integrated as a compositional element (not overlay). Color system: absolute black field, pure white, Phantom-red as the single accent, gold chrome as the second. The frame is simultaneously a film still and a concert poster from 1968 designed by someone who has memorized every Jean-Luc Godard credit sequence.

refs.name: Mirror's Edge — Clean Parkour Grammar
refs.use: Parkour city, high-key white environment, runner aesthetic, DICE visual grammar, freedom geometry
refs.dna: DICE's Mirror's Edge grammar: the rooftop city as high-key white canvas. The city of Glass, painted all-white by the administration, becomes the parkour runner's obstacle course and aesthetic manifesto. Climbable elements are marked with runner-red — pipes, ledges, vents — the safety grammar of a world where environment is the antagonist. Camera: first-person showing hands reaching for the next grab point. Heights reveal clean white geometry interrupted by red accents. Palette: white city, sky blue, runner red, the flesh-tone of Faith's hands. The visual argument: freedom is a geometry problem, and the city's perfection is the cage. The runner is the only color that was not commissioned.

refs.name: BioShock — Art Deco Decay Grammar
refs.use: Retrofuturism, submerged worlds, moral decay within elegance, 1940s architecture, art deco
refs.dna: Art Deco at the bottom of the sea: underwater-diffused light filtering through flooded observation windows onto ornate brass and mahogany architecture. The elegance is fractured — a ballroom occupied by a dead orchestra, a plasmid-sign still flickering neon-blue through algae, a welcome plaque in perfect 1940s lettering half-consumed by rust. Palette: deep ocean teal, warm amber ceiling light, cobalt neon, brass-brown architectural detail, blood-red warning light. The beauty makes the horror worse.

refs.name: Portal — Clean Lab Puzzle Grammar
refs.use: Aperture Science, clean white lab, portal technology, test chamber, Valve design grammar
refs.dna: Valve's Portal grammar: the Aperture Science test chamber as pristine white brutalism. The walls are panel-grid white, the floor concrete-grey, the ceiling holds camera turrets at perfect angles. The orange and blue portal openings are the only color in the frame — they read as violations of the space's law, portals to the same room from a different angle. GLaDOS's body hangs from the ceiling in the background, her silhouette a question. Palette: pure white, concrete grey, orange portal, blue portal, the occasional cake-brown hint. Chell's jumpsuit: muted orange against the white. The visual grammar of a clean space that is trying to kill you politely.

refs.name: Red Dead — Golden West Naturalism
refs.use: Western frontier, open landscape, nostalgia, freedom and its cost, period drama, isolation
refs.dna: Rockstar's cinematic west: ultra-wide shot of the Great Plains at golden hour, one mounted figure paused at a ridge — hat and coat silhouette against amber-red sky. Dust in the air diffracts the late sun. The land extends without a fence or road to the horizon in every direction. Palette: burnt amber-gold sky, sage-green prairie, red canyon sandstone, deep indigo shadow on the ground. The West as a place that is already ending — elegiac, not triumphant.

refs.name: The Last of Us — Overgrown Realism Grammar
refs.use: Post-apocalypse, nature reclaiming civilization, atmospheric horror, survival, plant biology
refs.dna: Naughty Dog's botanical horror: a formerly urban space completely reclaimed by vegetation — highway overpass draped in flowering vine, flooded basement with bioluminescent cordyceps bloom, supermarket aisles colonized by young forest. The horror is botanical, not monstrous; human civilization is the absent subject. Palette: deep green and brown-orange rust, filtered sunlight through vine canopy, pale grey concrete as the only memory of the human world. No human figure required — absence IS the content.

refs.name: Ghost of Tsushima — Wind Samurai Grammar
refs.use: Feudal Japan, wind aesthetic, sugi tree forest, warrior code, samurai honor
refs.dna: Sony/Sucker Punch's Ghost of Tsushima grammar: the wind as director — golden leaves or cherry blossoms directing the camera's attention through the environment. A samurai on horseback crossing a torii gate, the horizon a Hokusai-informed composition. The world is always a painting trying to escape being a game. Palette: autumn gold and red against pine-needle green, grey ocean cliff, steel-blue sword glint. The sun is always low, always warm, always creating dramatic sidelight. The wind system: particle leaves move in unified direction, indicating the shot's invisible guide. The frame holds: one samurai, one path, one decision.

refs.name: Death Stranding — Lonely Scale Grammar
refs.use: Vast empty landscape, lone figure, Kojima poetry, cargo weight, post-apocalyptic beauty
refs.dna: Hideo Kojima's Death Stranding grammar: a single figure with an impossibly large cargo load traversing a landscape of extreme beauty and absolute emptiness. The landscape is Iceland — volcanic black sand, moss-green tundra, waterfalls, overcast grey sky with occasional rainbow. Sam Porter Bridges is tiny against the environment, his cargo tower making him top-heavy, precarious, mortal. The rain is not weather but a metaphor — TIMEFALL aging everything it touches. Palette: Iceland grey, cargo orange, BT-black oil, baby-pink womb-space. The emotional register: loneliness as connection, isolation as love for a world that must be reconnected.

refs.name: God of War — Nordic Mythic Grammar
refs.use: Norse mythology, father-son relationship, winter forest, grounded fantasy, emotional weight
refs.dna: Santa Monica's grounded Norse epic: a figure at human height in a fjord forest at winter dusk — low sun just behind the treeline creating god-ray shafts through pine. Snow on the ground holds footprints. Breath is visible in cold air. Trees have root systems. Rocks have moss and age. The god of war is lit by the same light as the world; no separate hero lighting. Palette: cool blue-grey snow, pine green, rust-iron equipment, pale amber winter sun strip. Stillness before consequence.

refs.name: Horizon — Tech Nature Grammar
refs.use: Machines in nature, robot wildlife, overgrown future, Guerrilla visual grammar, lush world
refs.dna: Guerrilla Games' Horizon grammar: the impossible coexistence — a Thunderjaw mechanical dinosaur standing in a meadow overgrown with wildflowers, the machine's red eye scanning through lush grass. Human technology has become nature; nature has become more than it was. The Aloy in foreground: red hair against green environment, the machine's yellow caution-stripe visible at distance. The visual tension is always scale: the machine is always larger than the human. Palette: Horizon green (the world reclaimed), machine red-yellow warning, ancient ruin grey buried in vine, sky always impossibly blue and cloudless.

refs.name: Genshin — Elemental Fantasy Grammar
refs.use: Elemental magic, gacha hero, anime fantasy, miHoYo grammar, celestial world
refs.dna: miHoYo's Genshin Impact grammar: the Teyvat world as a gacha card come to life. Characters in elaborate fantasy-anime costumes at the center of an elemental effect — Pyro character surrounded by orbital fire particles, Cryo character in ice crystal radiance. The environment: Mondstadt wind cathedral, Liyue harbor at sunset, Inazuma lightning-island. Each region has its own color-temperature identity. Character silhouette: elaborate, highly differentiated, with weapon and element visible in one frame. The emotional register is JRPG wonder — the world is enormous, every horizon holds a dungeon, every dungeon holds a story.

refs.name: Minecraft — Block World Grammar
refs.use: Voxel world, Minecraft construction, biome identity, pixel landscape, creative freedom
refs.dna: Mojang's Minecraft grammar: the voxel world as architectural possibility. A landscape from 1-meter cubes: blocky trees, stepped mountains, flat-topped plateaus that are creative decisions rather than natural physics. The biome establishes the palette: jungle is deep green and vine-brown, desert is sandstone yellow, ocean monument is prismarine teal, mesa biome terracotta red. The view from above reveals the signature texture — the pixel pattern that communicates: this world was made by hand, one block at a time. At dusk: torches placed in a pattern both random and deliberate, their orange-amber against deepening night blue. The visual argument: any form is achievable through sufficient commitment to individual decisions.

refs.name: Animal Crossing — Soft Life Grammar
refs.use: Animal Crossing soft life, pastel island, Nintendo warmth, villager friendship, gentle time
refs.dna: Nintendo's Animal Crossing grammar: the island at golden hour as the ideal of gentle contentment. Villagers in their characteristic builds — the round, the elongated, the small — moving on personal schedules. Environment: soft-rounded trees, the turquoise of shallow-depth ocean, golden sand, the Museum dome as island landmark. Time changes the color: morning is fresh blue-gold, afternoon warm amber, evening peach, night star-filled purple. The visual argument: a life at nature's pace — one daily task, one friendship maintained, one fossil found — is sufficient. No urgency inhabits this island. Every season, every villager, every constellation is designed to communicate that you are exactly where you need to be.

refs.name: Stardew Valley — Cozy Pixel Grammar
refs.use: Cozy farming pixel, ConcernedApe art direction, pastoral pixel life, seasonal warmth
refs.dna: ConcernedApe's Stardew Valley pixel grammar: the farm at dawn as the opening frame — the farmer's house on the left, the crop rows extending right, seasonal color determining emotional register. The pixel art is 16x16 per tile but palette management achieves illustration-level readability. NPC interactions: character portraits in a window-box, hand-drawn expressions accompanying text. The 8-bit palette carries warmth through orange-amber primary and green secondary. The Joja corporation's steel-grey represents everything wrong. Villager schedules: each NPC walks their daily route, the world governed by natural time. The visual argument: good work, fresh air, and a modest plot of land compose a complete life.

refs.name: Celeste — Mountain Emotion Grammar
refs.use: Mental health, climbing as metaphor, pixel art, determination, emotional color grammar
refs.dna: Extremely OK's emotional pixel grammar: a tiny figure against an impossibly tall mountain face — previous stages visible far below, establishing the physical record of the climb. Pixel resolution is intentional, each pixel a deliberate compositional decision. Palette shifts with psychological state: cold blue-grey for dissociation, warm gold-rose for breakthrough, deep indigo-purple for depression, pink-magenta for self-acceptance. The mountain's architecture IS the emotional journey — impossible walls correspond to specific anxieties.

refs.name: Blade Runner — Neon Noir Grammar
refs.use: Dystopia, urban loneliness, technology vs humanity, detective noir, sci-fi atmosphere
refs.dna: Neo-noir rain city: deep blue-black void above, vertical rain catching neon (red, amber, blue) as falling curtains of light. Subject lit from above by a single neon source, rendering them as rim-lit silhouette with colored edge. Steam or smoke diffuses the mid-ground. Wet pavement doubles the neon geometry below as a mirror city. No warm key light — only cool or neon. Buildings are unreachably tall. The city is infinite, indifferent, and magnificent.

refs.name: Oppenheimer — Analog Density Grammar
refs.use: Historical weight, science, consequence, genius and destruction, analog authenticity
refs.dna: Nolan's IMAX analog texture: extreme close-up on physical matter — machined metal, glass lens, reactor graphite, human iris — filling the full frame with material information. Desaturated palette: warm amber, iron grey, sepia, bone. Practical fire, arc-welding spark, or nuclear light as the only source. No CGI. Each frame could be a 70mm still from 1945. An extreme close-up on one eye reflects the event. Sound and silence alternate; the visual information IS the score.

refs.name: Dune — Desert Monumental Grammar
refs.use: Epic scale, existential smallness, desert/wasteland, awe without aggression, sci-fi grandeur
refs.dna: Villeneuve's scale-as-awe: one figure registers as a pixel against a sand dune the size of a mountain range, or a sandworm fin rising from the desert floor like a continent. Horizon line impossibly high or low — no comfortable altitude. Bleached sky meeting sand at geometric angles. Palette: pale warm gold, cool blue shadow in dune valleys, overexposed sky. Zero motion blur — the universe is still and the human is small. Silence implied. The world is a cathedral that does not know you.

refs.name: Mad Max — Kinetic Wasteland Grammar
refs.use: Post-apocalyptic desert, vehicle carnage, wasteland orange, practical effects, George Miller grammar
refs.dna: George Miller's Fury Road grammar: the vehicle chase as opera. The desert is white salt flat or red ochre, the vehicles painted in rust and chrome and fire — mechanical modifications that communicate their owners' madness. The color temperature battle: wasteland orange-red against the electric blue of the pursuing night storm. Camera: mounted on the vehicles themselves, the horizon tilting as vehicles corner, the human bodies visible at the frame edge as scale. No CGI: the weight of a real vehicle at 100km/h is visible in every bouncing frame. The fuel quote is the moral framework: witness me.

refs.name: John Wick — Neon Precision Grammar
refs.use: Precision action, gun-fu choreography, nightclub/hotel environments, tactical elegance
refs.dna: Tactical choreography lit by practicals: a figure in black against high-contrast nightclub, hotel corridor, or continental rooftop. One neon key from above in a primary hue (electric blue, blood red, amber gold). Long shot holds the full body — no coverage cuts mid-sequence, one spatial understanding. Hard specular on wet floor. The camera is at rest while violence is in motion — the framing is architectural, not kinetic. Each action is legible, not blurred.

refs.name: Wes Anderson — Symmetry Grammar
refs.use: Deadpan comedy, nostalgia, obsessive order, coming-of-age, hotel/train/ship environments
refs.dna: Perfect bilateral symmetry about the vertical center line — camera level with subject eyeline, mounted on a horizontal slider, frontal. Every prop, person, and color swatch placed on a graphic grid. Color palette: one dominant pastel hue, one complementary accent, cream or white as the neutral field. Flat overhead fill with no motivated shadow — the world has no sun, only a production designer. Deadpan centered figures whose comedy arrives from perfect staging, never from expression. Each frame feels like a diorama under museum glass.

refs.name: Fincher — Controlled Green Noir
refs.use: Psychological thriller, corporate menace, hacker culture, control, obsession, digital darkness
refs.dna: Fincher's digital-age control: a desaturated teal-green color grade lowering ambient luminance, every surface rendered in its darkest photographic truth — black leather, wet concrete, server racks. Sharp focus across the full field with no creative bokeh. Subjects lit from above with minimal fill, creating strong under-eye shadow that reads as intelligence or danger. The camera moves with mechanical precision: exact rack, exact pan speed. Nothing is accidental. The world has been color-corrected to match the psychological state.

refs.name: Kubrick — One-Point Dread Grammar
refs.use: Institutional settings, authority, controlled dread, geometry as power, symmetry as threat
refs.dna: One-point perspective down the spine of a corridor, highway, or stairwell — every parallel line collapsing to a single vanishing point dead center. Locked camera, no handheld, no rack focus. Slow imperceptible push taking ten seconds to register. Subjects placed symmetrically flanking the recession spine, never blocking the vanishing point. Predominantly cool blue-grey or institutional green tonality, one warm anomaly (a lamp, a window). Dread emerges from geometric perfection, not from monster or violence. The frame feels like architecture dreamed it.

refs.name: Deakins — Naturalist Light Grammar
refs.use: Realism, character drama, prison/rural/domestic settings, craft invisibility, human truth
refs.dna: Deakins' invisible cinematography: motivated practical light only — one window, one lamp, one fire. No fill from off-screen sources unless motivated. Color temperature matches what the scene physically has: dawn is blue-grey, noon is harsh white, interior night is tungsten amber. Faces fully exposed but with real shadow depth. No cinematic grade — the image looks like reality but it is the best possible reality. The photography serves the actor, not the director's signature.

refs.name: Lubezki — Natural Long-Take Grammar
refs.use: Immersion, war sequences, magical realism, nature, consciousness-of-place, unbroken time
refs.dna: Lubezki's unbroken world: a single continuous take beginning inside a space and ending outside it — through windows, across open fields, through doorways — the camera flowing like a living thing at human shoulder height. Natural light only, meaning the image changes as clouds pass. No fixed focal length; the depth of field breathes with the camera's distance to subject. The world is real and the take proves it by never cutting away from it.

refs.name: Apple — Object Worship Grammar
refs.use: Product reveal, engineering minimalism, brand identity, desire-object staging, premium consumer
refs.dna: Apple's product theology: a single device in absolute void — one edge-light revealing the object geometry with surgical precision, a single perfect specular highlight traveling the curved surface from one edge to the other. Shadow footprint is clean and deliberate. No context, no hand, no user — the product is the world. The silence of the frame communicates that nothing else exists. Palette: pure white void or deep absolute black void. Product finish is the only texture.

refs.name: Nike — Kinetic Motivation Grammar
refs.use: Athletic achievement, motivational energy, physical peak, sports energy drink, kinetic product
refs.dna: Nike's peak biomechanics: an athlete at maximum physical extension — highest point of a jump, full extension of a sprint stride, peak torque of a pivot — frozen at the single frame where their body is at its absolute geometric limit. Hard directional key light from above creating a motion-implied shadow streak on the ground below. The body is a piece of kinetic sculpture. Palette: skin tone against white, black, or brand-specific color field. Minimal environment — one surface, one direction.

refs.name: Luxury Watch — Macro Precision Grammar
refs.use: Precision luxury, mechanical craftsmanship, macro material photography, Swiss engineering
refs.dna: Swiss watchmaking close-up: a 100mm macro lens on the rotor bridge or escapement wheel, filling the frame with mechanical precision. Extreme shallow depth of field — one gear tooth in sharp focus, the adjacent gear already soft. Light: single cold-white LED at low angle creating razor-edge specular on every metal surface. Background: absolute void. The watch case edge catches light as a thin luminous arc. No color — all silver, black, and the blue of blued steel.

refs.name: Beauty Skincare — Skin Glow Grammar
refs.use: Skincare, cosmetics, glow reveal, close-up skin texture, clean beauty, light as product
refs.dna: Beauty skincare grammar: extreme proximity — lens at 5cm from the skin surface, revealing pore texture and the precise moment hydration changes the surface optical quality. Light source: a single softbox from the 10 o'clock position, revealing depth through gentle shadow in the skin's natural topography. The glow is not a filter — it is actual light on actual skin, a physical fact that the frame documents. Palette: skin tone as primary, clean white or nude background, one neutral accent. Movement, if any: a single water droplet making contact with the surface. The product is proof of light.

refs.name: Food Tabletop Macro Grammar
refs.use: Food macro, tabletop product, appetite texture, studio ingredient beauty, culinary desire
refs.dna: Food tabletop macro grammar: the camera is 3 centimeters from the subject — steam rising from espresso, condensation beading on a cold glass, the exact moment a knife reveals the interior cross-section of an ingredient. Light from a 45-degree angle at the front quarter, revealing food surface texture with the specificity of a scientific instrument. The table surface: natural stone, warm wood grain, linen — each material reads its temperature back to the viewer. The appetite moment is one frame: pour, cut, lift, first-condensation. Hold on it. Let the viewer want it before it moves.

refs.name: Architecture Window Light Grammar
refs.use: Architectural photography, interior space, window-light spatial reveal, residential quality
refs.dna: Architectural photography grammar: a residential or commercial interior shot from a corner at approximately 24mm, one window visible at mid-frame providing the key light. The room's geometry — ceiling plane, floor plane, wall corners — converges naturally in the frame, establishing the architecture's spatial logic. Furniture positioned to reveal the spatial narrative: a reading chair placed where the light is, a dining table oriented toward the window. The palette is whatever the room contains, but the light source is always natural. The occupant is absent but implied: a half-read book, a coat on a hook. The frame argues: architecture is not about buildings but about the quality of light and space it creates for human life.

refs.name: Tech Clinical Glass Grammar
refs.use: Medical technology, clinical precision, clean-room aesthetic, healthcare product, trust design
refs.dna: Medical technology visual grammar: a clinical environment rendered to communicate trust through precision. The device on a surface that implies sterile quality: hard white, brushed stainless steel, glass. The human hand is present but gloved — the interaction is professional, the technology is serious. Lighting: high-key from all directions, eliminating shadow as a signal of clinical standards. The device's interface: clear, readable at a glance, life-critical information communicated without decoration. Palette: white-white-grey-stainless plus one healthcare-specific accent (often blue, sometimes green — the colors of trust in medical contexts). The visual argument: this device was made to the standards of things that matter.

refs.name: Spider-Verse — Miles Morales Energy Anchor
refs.use: graffiti color bursts, black-red hero contrast, comic halftone, leap/fall rhythm, urban youth energy
refs.avoid: Miles suit/logo/webs/exact face, Marvel symbols, exact shot copy
refs.dna: black-red hero silhouette under pressure from below — Ben-Day halftone dots layered on urban rooftop staging, graffiti cyan and neon pink accent burst framing the lead edge, kinetic diagonal falling camera locked on the subject's silhouette mass, speed-smear impact lines radiating from the point of peak force

refs.name: Spider-Verse — Gwen Pastel Punk Anchor
refs.use: Gwen Spider-Verse watercolor, pink-teal palette, offset register, pastel energy, music topic
refs.dna: Spider-Verse's Ghost-Spider visual grammar for emotional creative content: watercolor wash backgrounds in soft pink-teal-white, bleeding at object edges as if still wet. The ink outlines of characters are clean but the painted fill is loose. Slight offset chromatic registration — deliberate misalignment giving the impression the image was printed twice on slightly different paper positions. Pastel radial spotlight on the subject, flat pop-art color areas. The palette reads: creativity, music, performance, emotional honesty — the visual grammar of an artwork that knows it is an artwork and is proud of it. Motion (if any): the background washes pulse one time with the music.

refs.name: Bleach — Hollow Mask Pressure
refs.use: Hollow mask transformation, inner hollow drama, dual identity, spiritual pressure, Ichigo grammar
refs.avoid: mask design copy, Ichigo face/outfit, exact sword
refs.dna: Tite Kubo's Hollow mask grammar: the moment of transformation — the Hollow mask forming over the protagonist's face, one eye visible through the mask's single empty socket, the other lost in shadow. The spiritual pressure is visible as black energy with orange lightning emanating outward, warping the environment. The duality: the white Hollow mask against the black Shinigami robe, the orange hair splitting both. The composition is always extreme: full-length shot establishing the transformation's scale, then close on the mask as it locks into place. Palette: black-white-orange as the Ichigo system, with the Hollow mask adding its specific bone-white against everything else. The fight has changed register.

refs.name: One Piece — Grand Line Scale
refs.use: Grand Line ocean scale, island arriving, epic sea scope, Oda visual ambition, adventure approaching
refs.avoid: crew likeness, Jolly Roger/logo, Sunny exact design
refs.dna: Eiichiro Oda's Grand Line scale grammar: the island reveal — the moment the Thousand Sunny approaches a new landmass that fills the frame horizon. The island is always extraordinary: a city floating on clouds, a jungle island with a mountain god, a winter island in summer seas, an undersea kingdom. The crew on deck in silhouette against the approaching landmass, their collective excitement readable in posture. Scale: the island must be visibly larger than the ship. The sky: the Grand Line's weather is extreme — cherry blossoms falling in a snowstorm, two seasons visible from the same deck. The visual promise of One Piece: every horizon will be exceeded by what lies beyond it.

refs.name: Chainsaw Man — Urban Grit Grammar
refs.use: Urban supernatural horror, chainsaw violence aesthetic, Fujimoto grammar, raw graphic energy
refs.dna: Tatsuki Fujimoto's Chainsaw Man grammar: the visual texture of cheap newsprint — slightly rough, as if the image was printed once and scanned. Urban Japan at night: convenience store lighting, dingy apartment hallways, the specific fluorescent light of a city that never fully turns off. Blood is matter-of-fact, not theatrical — it lands where physics demands. The Chainsaw Devil in active state: chainsaws erupting from head and arms, the body a joyful tool for destruction. Denji's expression: open, simple, happy in a way that should be deeply disturbing. The horror and the comedy occupy exactly the same frame.

refs.name: Spy x Family — Pastel Comedy Grammar
refs.use: Pastel comedy, spy family warmth, Tatsuya Endo grammar, domestic comedy with secrets
refs.dna: Tatsuya Endo's Spy × Family grammar: the comedy of normality maintained under impossible conditions. The Forger family at a dining table, each member secretly performing — Loid as a spy performing fatherhood, Yor performing domesticity while an assassin, Anya reading everyone's thoughts and finding it hilarious. The visual tone is warm pastel comedy: soft pink-cream palette, round expressive faces, exaggerated micro-expressions that hold one frame too long. The humor is always situational — no one laughs in the scene, the reader laughs at the scene. Anya's deadpan expression is the anchor of everything.

refs.name: Frieren — Melancholy Fantasy Journey
refs.use: Elven melancholy, long memory, peaceful magic, slow fantasy, Yamada Kanehito grammar
refs.dna: Yamada Kanehito's Frieren grammar: the quiet aftermath of heroism. A thousand-year-old elf walking through a forest where she once fought a demon, now peaceful, her memory longer than any human relationship. The visual pace is slow — frames held 3 seconds longer than necessary. Magic in this world is gentle: leaves lifted on invisible air currents, light gathering at fingertips. Palette: soft autumn colors, silver hair against muted landscape greens, the particular grey of overcast European-fantasy skies.
