The emotion is always retrospective — every scene is someone remembering what has already passed.

refs.name: Pokémon — Bright Creature-World Grammar
refs.use: Pokémon friendship, bright adventure, creature partnership, optimistic world, Ken Sugimori grammar
refs.avoid: Pokémon designs, Pokéball/logo, trainer exact outfits
refs.dna: Ken Sugimori and Game Freak's Pokémon visual grammar: a trainer and their partner Pokémon in the moment before a contest or journey — the relationship between human and creature is the emotional center. The world is always bright: saturated primary colors in clean flat areas (grass, sky, water). The Pokémon is at full silhouette readability — recognizable in one look, a design principle as strong as any logo system. The trainer's expression is always open, forward-facing, determined without aggression. No violence visible, only potential. The visual promise: friendship with a creature is possible, and it will take you anywhere.

refs.name: Yu-Gi-Oh! — Duel Stage Drama
refs.use: Card game duel, holographic monster, ancient power, Egyptian mythology, dramatic reveal
refs.dna: Kazuki Takahashi's Yu-Gi-Oh! duel grammar: the dueling arena as theater — two players across from each other, the holographic monsters they've summoned towering above them at 50 meters scale. The monster reveal is always a silhouette-first moment: dark outline against a bright backdrop, then the full reveal on cut. Ancient Egyptian iconography and geometric energy patterns surround the high-level monsters. Palette: dark dramatic sky, gold Egyptian accent, the specific orange of Duel Monsters holograms. The stakes of a card game rendered with the visual language of the end of the world.

refs.name: Ghibli — Bathhouse Warmth Grammar
refs.use: Spirit world bathhouse, supernatural economy, Chihiro small against enormous scale, Miyazaki
refs.dna: Miyazaki's Spirited Away grammar: the bathhouse as supernatural economy running on rules the viewer intuits but cannot articulate. Steam rising from multiple floors, lanterns at every window, spirit customers arriving by boat from the dark sea beyond. Chihiro in white work-uniform is always the smallest figure in any frame she occupies — the bathhouse enormous, the spirits enormous, only the girl and viewer are human scale. Colors: lantern-warm amber against midnight blue, the teal-green of spirit-world water, Yubaba's office in accumulated gold. Every spirit guest is a mystery the camera honors by not explaining. The bath tokens, the work schedule, the name stolen — the bureaucracy of the afterlife is completely legible even to a 10-year-old, and that is the achievement.

refs.name: Dragon Flight Adventure Grammar
refs.use: Dragon flight, Nordic sky, Toothless night fury, aerial wonder, DreamWorks scale
refs.dna: Dean DeBlois' How to Train Your Dragon flight grammar: the relationship between rider and dragon in aerial space. Hiccup prone on Toothless's back, the rider's palm flat as wind-brake, the dragon's pupils dilating with speed changes. The camera follows but also strays: pull-back reveals the entire archipelago below, the village a toy. Sky: morning mist on fjord water, cloud breaks revealing gold, aurora borealis at altitude. The visual emotion: flight as perfect trust — a human lying flat on the back of a dragon that could kill them, and both of them choosing to go faster. The Nordic palette: grey-green water, amber firelight from the village, Toothless night-black against everything else.

refs.name: Kung Fu Panda — Brush Action Grammar
refs.use: Wuxia animation, ink-brush action, Chinese landscape, master-student drama, DreamWorks east
refs.dna: DreamWorks' Kung Fu Panda brush grammar: the chinoiserie approach — Chinese landscape painting as animation backdrop. A sequence of mountains (Jade Palace approach) rendered in traditional ink-wash with modern 3D depth. Po's action sequences: the moment his weight becomes an advantage, the visual grammar shifts — slow motion, ink-splash speed lines, the panda body at unexpected grace. Palette: red and gold of the Jade Palace against mountain fog grey, Kai's jade-fire green, the specific warm amber of a kung fu training sequence at sunset. The visual argument: mastery is the moment when your nature stops fighting you.

refs.name: Coco — Marigold Memory Grammar
refs.use: Día de los Muertos, marigold bridge, Land of the Dead, memory and family, Pixar cultural
refs.dna: Lee Unkrich's Coco grammar: the marigold petal bridge as the most beautiful thing in the frame — twenty meters wide, warm orange-amber petals flowing from the Land of the Dead to the Land of the Living. The Land of the Dead: a vertical city of stacked colored buildings at night, warm light from every window, the scale impossible and welcoming. Miguel in the middle distance, small against the afterlife city's grandeur. Palette: marigold orange as the warmth axis, deep night blue as the cold axis, and between them: the entire spectrum of the Mexican-tile city. Memory is color; forgetting is grey.

refs.name: Inside Out — Emotion Space Grammar
refs.use: Emotion personification, mind architecture, Pixar abstract, color as character identity
refs.dna: Pete Docter's Inside Out grammar: the emotion characters as color-coded psychology. Joy: yellow-blue glow, literal luminescence. Sadness: blue-grey, drooping posture, the world a little darker when she's touching it. Fear: purple, tall and thin, legs ready to run. Each emotion has a body language that communicates their function before they speak. The Headquarters: a glass-and-light console, memory orbs in globes lining every wall, the personality islands visible through windows. Palette: the emotion's own color extends into the environment around them. The visual grammar of the mind: everything is a metaphor, and the metaphors are physical.

refs.name: WALL-E — Lonely Robot Scale Grammar
refs.use: Robot loneliness, silent film grammar, environmental warning, Pixar minimalism, space solitude
refs.dna: Andrew Stanton's WALL-E grammar: the first act as a silent film of loneliness. WALL-E alone in a city of garbage towers, the Earth silent except for his motor and the music from the Hello Dolly VHS. The binoculars as eyes: framing anything he's curious about. EVE arriving: hard white against his warm brown, her design as alien as any object but immediately legible as feminine through posture alone. Space sequence: the Earth's blue-white against black, two small robots in the void holding hands. The visual argument: loneliness is the condition, connection is the event. The entire first act is in pantomime, and it is enough.

refs.name: LEGO Movie — Brick Energy Grammar
refs.use: Meta animation, LEGO physics, stop-motion-CG hybrid, anarchic creativity, fourth-wall grammar
refs.dna: Phil Lord and Christopher Miller's LEGO Movie grammar: everything is made of LEGO, including the water, the smoke, and the explosions — no particle physics, only brick physics. The 'hand-built' quality: in the action sequences, the camera shakes as if someone is holding the set. The meta-layer: Emmett and the world of Taco Tuesday vs. the Master Builder anarchism. Palette: LEGO primary color system at 100% saturation, plus the trans-neon orange of the Piece of Resistance. The visual argument: everything is awesome because everything can be taken apart and rebuilt into something better. Including the film's own genre conventions.

refs.name: SpongeBob — Flat Absurd Comedy Grammar
refs.use: Underwater absurdism, flat color comedy, Bikini Bottom humor, Hillenburg design
refs.dna: Stephen Hillenburg's SpongeBob grammar: the cartoon logic of Bikini Bottom where underwater physics are applied only when they provide a comedic opportunity. SpongeBob's face: a simple grid of holes on a yellow square, capable of expressing the full range of human emotion through eyebrow position alone. The backgrounds: painted watercolor with a warm afternoon quality, coral and kelp as domestic architecture. The FUN-O-METER of emotional escalation: each comedic beat is one step more absurd than the last until physical law dissolves entirely. Palette: SpongeBob yellow, Patrick pink, Squidward teal-grey, the specific aqua of Bikini Bottom light. The visual argument: nothing needs to make sense as long as the timing is right.

refs.name: Samurai Jack — Minimal Epic Grammar
refs.use: Genndy Tartakovsky graphic minimalism, samurai honor, negative space, stylized violence
refs.dna: Genndy Tartakovsky's Samurai Jack grammar: the negative space as protagonist. A lone samurai figure against a vast background that might be sky, field, or abstract color — the subject occupies 10% of the frame, the 90% around him is the weight of consequence. Action sequences: reduced to silhouette, the limbs and sword as graphic arcs rather than rendered figures. Palette: the limited color system of '60s Japanese graphic design — one warm tone, one cool tone, one neutral, black line, white ground. The emotional register is mono no aware: the beauty of transience. Jack's expression is always stoic, always already past the emotion he is experiencing.

refs.name: Batman TAS — Deco Noir Grammar
refs.use: Art deco Gotham, Bruce Timm design, noir shadow grammar, villain elegance, DC animated
refs.dna: Bruce Timm and Paul Dini's Batman animated grammar: the Art Deco city as moral landscape. Gotham City's skyscrapers have the silhouette of Fritz Lang's Metropolis and the color palette of Max Fleischer's Superman — angular, heavy, compressed. Batman as pure shadow: the figure is always backlit, the cape is always a volume, the cowl's eyes are always white in shadow. Villain design: the Joker's white skin and purple suit as color theory — complementary colors at maximum saturation, the color of a carnival that has gone wrong. The visual rule: the hero must always be slightly smaller than the architecture they inhabit. The city is the real protagonist.

refs.name: League/Arcane — Bridge Grammar
refs.use: Game-to-animation translation, Piltover industrial fantasy, Fortiche painterly 3D, bridge grammar
refs.dna: Fortiche Production's bridge grammar between League of Legends and animation: Piltover's industrial-fantasy architecture translated from game-engine geometry into painterly 3D with hand-applied brush-stroke albedo on every surface. Teal-amber warm-cool colour temperature war defines the entire visual space — teal for the established and powerful, ember-orange for the threatened and resistant. Brush-stroke texture on every material: stone has visible paint marks, metal carries pigment traces, skin is painted rather than rendered. Characters are recognizable to League players and legible to newcomers simultaneously. The bridge function is the achievement: fan service and cinematic quality, neither sacrificing the other.

refs.name: Warcraft — Epic Fantasy Scale
refs.use: Horde vs Alliance, high fantasy epic, Blizzard world-building, classic WoW drama
refs.dna: Blizzard's World of Warcraft grammar: the hero shot of iconic faction architecture — Orgrimmar's iron-spired gates at sunset, Stormwind's cathedral quarter in morning blue. Faction identity through pure design language: Horde brutalist iron and leather and bone, Alliance Gothic stone and blue-gold banner. The racial design differences: Orc heavyset brutalist physicality against Human proportional heroism. Epic scale: buildings are always three times what they need to be, because this is a world of legend, not proportion. Cinematic palette: warm amber for Horde firelight, cold Dalaran blue for Alliance magic. The visual promise: this world has been here for ten thousand years.

refs.name: StarCraft — Military Sci-Fi Grammar
refs.use: Space military, Zerg swarm, Terran bunker, epic space warfare, Blizzard sci-fi
refs.dna: Blizzard's StarCraft grammar: the three-faction visual war. Terran: industrial military grey with orange-yellow construction markings, human scale in enormous machines. Zerg: organic-biological horror, chitinous purple-black, swarm scale — a hundred units visible as one moving mass. Protoss: golden crystalline architecture, psionic blue energy, ancient dignity. The scale is always beyond individual combat: planetary invasion, orbital assault, the Overmind consuming a world. Composition: the human soldier is always small against the Zerg swarm or the Protoss colossus. The military fonts and UI design carry the Terran's blue-collar space-trucker aesthetic.

refs.name: Diablo — Dark Gothic Loot Grammar
refs.use: Dark gothic dungeon, demon horror, Sanctuary world, Blizzard gothic grammar
refs.dna: Blizzard's Diablo grammar: the gothic dungeon as the setting for all moral questions. The Nephalem hero is always small against the dungeon architecture — cathedral ceilings that disappear into shadow, monster hordes that outnumber comprehension. Fire is the primary light source: red-orange flame revealing the dungeon's stone walls in staccato shadow. The demons: enormous, geometrically wrong, designed to feel anatomically incorrect. Palette: dungeon grey-black, blood red, hellfire orange, the rare blue of a magic item's glow. The emotional register: the horror is not fear but resolve — the hero has chosen to enter this place, and they will descend further still.

refs.name: World of Warcraft — Zone Color Grammar
refs.use: Zone identity, faction color grammar, open world fantasy, Azeroth exploration
refs.dna: Blizzard's WoW zone grammar: each zone as a self-contained color identity so strong it communicates itself without icons. Elwynn Forest: warm morning greens, golden sunbeams through oak canopy. Durotar: arid red-rock desert, sparse scrub, fierce midday sun. Ashenvale: deep night-purple, silver moonlight through ancient wood. Tanaris: yellow-white desert at noon. The zone transition is always visible in the palette: one horizon of amber Barrens savanna visible from the green edge of Mulgore. The minimap is the frame: every zone is a painting with its own color theory. Players navigate by palette.

refs.name: Final Fantasy — Crystal Melodrama Grammar
refs.use: Crystal motif, JRPG elegance, Yoshitaka Amano visual, summon spectacle, world myth
refs.dna: Yoshitaka Amano and Square Enix's Final Fantasy grammar: the crystal as metaphysical center — a floating crystalline structure shedding white-blue light that reaches every corner of the frame. Character design in the Amano tradition: flowing garments, impossibly long limbs, elaborate detail that couldn't survive motion but exists in concept perfection. The summon sequence: a figure assembling from light particles at enormous scale, wings or elemental forces completing the form. Palette: crystal white and blue as base, with each game's accent — FFVII advent-grey, FFXIV Azim Steppe gold, FFX Zanarkand amber. The frame is always mythological: this is religion rendered in light.

refs.name: Kingdom Hearts — Light/Dark Whimsy
refs.use: Light vs darkness, keyblade, Disney collaboration, JRPG heart motif, Nomura grammar
refs.dna: Tetsuya Nomura's Kingdom Hearts grammar: the Keyblade as the moral axis — the weapon is also a key, and keys open, not destroy. The Organization XIII cloaked figures in black against white light. Disney world locations rendered in anime aesthetic: Agrabah's desert heat, Neverland's blue sky night, Hollow Bastion's gothic ruin. The heart motif: visible in architecture (heart-shaped windows), in particle effects (hearts scatter upward when something ends). Palette: darkness as pure black void against Kingdom light (warm amber-gold). Sora in red-black against everything, because he is always the exception to the rule.

refs.name: Metal Gear — Tactical Espionage Grammar
refs.use: Stealth military, Kojima cinema, tactical espionage, CQC grammar, soldier philosophy
refs.dna: Hideo Kojima's Metal Gear grammar: the tactical espionage reality as cinema. Snake in active camo against an industrial environment — a nuclear disposal site, a jungle outpost, an Arctic research station. The cinematic codec call: the close-up portrait against a black background, the call-sign name appearing character by character. The codec conversations are the game's philosophy delivered in extreme close-up. Palette: cold military grey, jungle green, nuclear-warning yellow, the specific grey of an AK-74 in poor light. The cardboard box: the absurdity that the enemy will not question if you believe in it hard enough. The visual grammar of one man against a world that demands he be more than human.

refs.name: Resident Evil — Survival Horror Grammar
refs.use: Survival horror, tight corridor, limited ammo, resource management tension, Capcom horror
refs.dna: Capcom's Resident Evil grammar: the corridor that might be safe — the camera positioned at the end of a hallway, the player's perspective small against the architectural space. A door at the far end. Flashlight beam illuminating 3 meters in a dark that continues beyond. The horror is acoustic and spatial: the Crimson Head visible at frame edge, or not. Palette: survival grey-black dungeon, emergency red lighting, the greenish-yellow of an herb pickup, the precise brown of a wooden storage box that means safety. Resource scarcity is visual: the ammo counter in the corner is the most important number in the frame.

refs.name: Silent Hill — Fog Psychological Horror
refs.use: Psychological horror, fog world, town limbo, Akira Yamaoka grammar, existential dread
refs.dna: Konami/Team Silent's Silent Hill grammar: the fog as a mercy — the horror is not visible until it is too late. A figure on a town street where the fog begins 10 meters ahead, the buildings behind them perfectly visible, the road ahead dissolving into white. The alternate world (Otherworld) uses rust and chain and industrial darkness. The sirens signal the transition — not monster but architecture of dread. Palette: fog-white obscures everything; the few colors that cut through are significant: the protagonist's red jacket, a child's drawing pinned to a wall, the rust-red of the Otherworld. The radio static is louder than any monster.

refs.name: Uncharted — Pulp Adventure Realism
refs.use: Pulp adventure, treasure hunt, Naughty Dog cinematic, action archaeology, globe-trotting
refs.dna: Naughty Dog's Uncharted grammar: the photorealistic adventure pulp. Nathan Drake in motion — running from something enormous (collapsing building, train car, flooded temple), the frame always indicating what approaches from behind while Drake looks at what's ahead. The environments are the star: Cartagena's rooftops, Nepal's village streets, pirate coves in Madagascar. Palette: warm adventure amber-gold for the treasure moments, urgent environment-specific color for the action. The humor lives in Drake's expression: mild annoyance in the middle of impossible physical situations. Naughty Dog's rule: the camera never loses sight of the character's face.

refs.name: Assassin’s Creed — Historical Parkour Grammar
refs.use: Historical setting, parkour assassin, Animus aesthetic, Ubisoft grammar, eagle vision
refs.dna: Ubisoft's Assassin's Creed grammar: the historical city as a parkour course. Altair on Jerusalem's rooftops — white robe against Crusader-era stone, the eagle's-eye view from a Viewpoint tower revealing the city's grid. The hidden blade at the precise angle of the assassination. Eagle Vision: the world rendered in lapis blue for the environment, gold for allies, red for enemies — the Animus's attempt to decode human intention through color. Each historical period has its period-correct palette: Venetian Renaissance gold and canal reflection, Ancient Egypt warm sandstone, Greek blue-white marble. The visual promise: history can be inhabited, not just observed.

refs.name: The Last of Us Part II — Grounded Drama
refs.use: Post-apocalyptic drama, overgrown Seattle, moral grey, Naughty Dog realism, violence weight
refs.dna: Naughty Dog's The Last of Us Part II grammar: the violence has weight because the characters have weight. Ellie in the overgrown Pacific Northwest — Seattle's downtown reclaimed by forest, the Space Needle visible through fir trees. The color of this world: wet green moss, grey concrete, the specific ochre of aged fluorescent lighting in abandoned spaces. The flashback to Jackson: warm amber, safety, loss implied by contrast. The guitar: the only beautiful thing in a frame that otherwise documents consequence. The rule: if the violence is difficult to watch, you are watching correctly. No catharsis is offered. Only aftermath.

refs.name: Adventure Ruins Gold Grammar
refs.use: Ancient treasure chamber, golden light revelation, adventure archaeology, discovery moment
refs.dna: The treasure chamber reveal: warm amber-gold light from torch or shaft of sunlight breaking through ancient stone, illuminating a chamber that has not been entered in 500 years. The first sight: gold piled floor-to-ceiling, jade statues, the scale visible through the explorers in the foreground doorway. The camera is at threshold — one foot in the known world, one in the legendary. The gold reflects back the explorers' torch, making them golden too. The visual promise of adventure archaeology: the thing you have been looking for actually exists and it is here. Dust motes in the shaft of light. Hold 4 seconds before anyone speaks.

refs.name: Open-World Satire City Grammar
refs.use: American satire, Los Santos/Los Angeles, Rockstar grammar, luxury alongside poverty
refs.dna: Rockstar Games' GTA grammar: the American city as hyper-real satire. Los Santos at golden hour — the Hollywood Hills mansions visible from the ocean highway, palm trees against a smog-orange sunset, luxury vehicles on a freeway also carrying garbage trucks. The world is beautiful and corrupt in the same frame. The character designs: exaggerated but recognizable social archetypes. The radio is the moral voice: commercials advertising things that explain the crime happening below. The visual argument: you are looking at a world that exists, just slightly turned up. The satirical distance: exactly zero.

refs.name: Gran Turismo — Precision Car Beauty
refs.use: Motorsport precision, vehicle art, circuit beauty, automotive photography, Polyphony grammar
refs.dna: Polyphony Digital's Gran Turismo grammar: automotive photography elevated to art direction. The vehicle alone in a location that exists to honor it — the Nürburgring in morning mist, Fuji Speedway in winter light, the Goodwood motor circuit in English overcast. The car is the subject; the landscape is the frame. Brake disc glow visible in the long-exposure motion. Precision detail: tire sidewall lettering, brake caliper color, badge reflection. Palette: the vehicle's own color is primary, the environment's light is secondary. The visual argument: a vehicle is beautiful in the same way a building is beautiful — proportions, materials, the evidence of engineering thought made visible.

refs.name: Basketball Arena Premium Grammar
refs.use: Basketball arena, court scale, premium sports, athlete heroism, broadcast drama
refs.dna: NBA premium visual grammar: the arena at full capacity — 20,000 people in darkness around a rectangle of hardwood that exists only in light. The court is 18,000 square feet of stage, and the athlete is the performer at center stage. Camera: low, wide, looking up at the shot arc. The ball at the peak of its trajectory against the arena's upper darkness. Sound is visible in the frame — the crowd's energy bends the atmosphere. Palette: each team's court colors as foundation, arena lighting in warm-white against darkness above. The visual argument: sport is theater, and the arena is the oldest form of theater.

refs.name: Minimal EV Tech Grammar
refs.use: Electric vehicle, clean tech, minimal environment, futurism, silent motion
refs.dna: Tesla's visual grammar of electrical silence: the vehicle gliding through a white desert or minimal urban geometry with no engine-sound reference — the speed is visual only, communicated through motion blur and the clean geometry of the frame. Interior: the touchscreen as the dashboard altar, minimal controls as philosophy-made-physical. Exterior: one smooth surface interrupted only by door handles and light signatures. Palette: white, silver, deep grey, electric blue accent. The brand argument is not speed or luxury but a different relationship to the machine itself — software as vehicle, vehicle as software.

refs.name: Dyson-Style Airflow Grammar
refs.use: Product engineering reveal, airflow visualization, premium tech, clean industrial design
refs.dna: Dyson's engineering reveal: airflow made visible through colored smoke or CGI particle streams — the fluid dynamics of the motor visible as organized laminar-flow paths moving through the product and out the nozzle. Technical cross-section: the interior mechanism revealed by a 'glass wall' or exploded-view moment. Palette: white product shell, cool blue-tinted airflow visualization, grey or dark-studio background. The technology IS the aesthetic.

refs.name: Luxury Watch Macro Grammar
refs.use: Heritage luxury, achievement, precision material, gifting context, aspirational brand identity
refs.dna: Rolex's object permanence: the Oyster case on a perfectly neutral surface, a single large-format key light from above-left at 45 degrees, creating one precise specular highlight that travels the case curve from bezel to lug. The date magnifier lens catches its own miniature world-in-reflection. Palette: warm champagne gold, cool rhodium silver, deep blue or black dial. The watch does not move. It does not need to — it communicates through absolute stillness.

refs.name: Red Beverage Refresh Grammar
refs.use: Summer refreshment, appetite desire, classic brand warmth, beverage close-up staging
refs.dna: Classic Coke desire staging: a glass bottle with condensation drops running down the surface at golden backlight — the light passes through the amber liquid and the glass neck simultaneously, creating a warm concentrated glow at the bottle's center. The red label catches the backlight as a saturated warm band. A hand closes around the bottle creating a grip-impression in the condensation. Palette: Coca-Cola red, warm amber-gold light, white condensation, neutral warm background.

refs.name: Warm Café Lifestyle Grammar
refs.use: Café warmth, comfort, morning ritual, creative space, social connection, slow beverage moments
refs.dna: Third-place comfort staging: a ceramic latte-art cup at a wood-grain table, steam curling from the surface in one continuous upward S-curve. Key light from above-left, warm tungsten quality, catching the coffee surface and creating a soft shadow footprint. Bokeh background of café practicals (amber orbs, out-of-focus wooden shelves). The cup is held by hands — not studio floating. Palette: dark espresso brown, steamed-milk white, warm café amber, natural wood grain.

refs.name: Pop Fast-Food Grammar
refs.use: Food appetite, fast-food scale, burger close-up, QSR commercial grammar, pop-food energy
refs.dna: Appetite staging at golden hour: a sesame-seed bun slightly above eye level, shot with a 50mm at f/2 — the sesame seeds sharp, the lettuce edge soft, the cheese pull still in motion below. Warm key light from above creating specular on the bun's top surface. Background: clean warm yellow-red chromatic suggestion, no specific set. The color temperature is 3200K, pushed warmer in grade. Every element exists to make you hungry in 0.5 seconds.

refs.name: Spider-Verse Miles Grammar
refs.use: Miles Morales Spider-Verse identity, Brooklyn energy, graffiti aesthetic, Afro-Latino hero grammar
refs.avoid: Spider-Man character, webs, Marvel logos, Miles Morales likeness
refs.dna: Sony Pictures Animation's Miles Morales visual grammar: the Brooklyn wall mural as the identity statement — subway aerosol tags and Williamsburg murals as the environment Miles navigates on his way to becoming Spider-Man. The Ben-Day dot halftone patterns appearing on figures and backgrounds during peak emotional moments. Miles' design: the Brooklyn Visions Academy uniform underneath the spray-paint-customized Spider-Man suit, the Jordans. The color system: Miles' Spider-suit is red-black vs Peter's red-blue, the distinction carrying the full weight of identity. When Miles leaps: the comic panels arrange around him, his specific leap angle — a fall that becomes a choice — the defining motion.

refs.name: Spider-Verse Noir 1940s
refs.use: Spider-Noir black-and-white dimension, 1930s detective, hard-boiled visual, monochrome style
refs.avoid: Spider-Noir character, film noir IP copy
refs.dna: Spider-Verse's Noir visual grammar: a dimension where color does not exist. The 1930s New York of Spider-Man Noir is rendered entirely in black, white, and deep shadow grey — as if a newspaper photograph come to life. The trench coat and fedora are the only characterization needed. Rain on brick walls, fire-escape silhouettes, a single motivating streetlamp. The visual logic: in this dimension, everything looks like a crime scene photograph because everything is, in some way, a crime scene. The only color that leaks through is the spider-symbol — which exists in red even in Noir's universe, as if the rules of that dimension cannot fully contain it.

refs.name: Demon Slayer ufotable Grammar
refs.use: Ufotable cinematic technique, UFOTABLE water flame FX, Taisho anime prestige grammar
refs.avoid: Actual Demon Slayer characters (Tanjiro, Nezuko, etc.), breathing form names, Mugen Train
refs.dna: Ufotable's Demon Slayer cinematic grammar: the visual FX technique that redefined TV anime production. The Flame Breathing sequence: discrete flame shapes stacked and animated individually, not as particle simulation but as hand-composited elements layered at high frame rate. The Water Breathing traces: curved 3D water surfaces animated as solid forms, not splash, following the sword arc in geometric precision. The Mugen Train sky: photographic cloud movement underneath cel-animated foreground figures. The color treatment: the sky is always dramatically colored at the moment of peak battle — sunrise amber, twilight purple, emergency red. The standard: every background is a location-scouted photograph painted over. The production value is the argument.

refs.name: JJK Cursed Energy Grammar
refs.use: MAPPA JJK animation prestige, cursed energy technique, Gege manga vs MAPPA screen grammar
refs.avoid: Gojo, Itadori, actual Domain Expansion names, Cursed Spirit designs
refs.dna: MAPPA's Jujutsu Kaisen animated grammar: the adaptation of Gege Akutami's loose, energetic manga line into high-production TV animation. The Domain Expansion sequences: full black void with a specific environment materializing around both combatants — Gojo's Infinity as the visual grammar of a space where light bends. Cursed technique visualization: each sorcerer's ability has a specific graphic signature (Nobara's nails traveling in camera-follow, Megumi's shadow domain as a flat black floor generating shadow animals). The Shibuya Incident: multiple characters in crisis simultaneously, the animation tracking between them with deliberate jump-cut rhythm. The color grade: slightly desaturated modern CG, not the saturated cel of earlier anime. Realism serving the supernatural.

refs.name: Arcane × Clay Hybrid
refs.use: Clay material meets prestige painterly animation, tactile fantasy world, textured dark drama
refs.avoid: Actual Arcane/League IP, dark/violent tone — keep educational warmth
refs.dna: Fortiche's painterly brush on clay-like material surfaces: the Arcane visual grammar of oil-paint texture strokes applied to characters rendered with the physical depth of stop-motion clay or felt. Every surface has both the painted quality of Zaun's industrial grime AND the tactile physical reality of a handmade object you could hold. Lighting emphasizes depth of material — key light creates shadow that reveals the clay thickness, not just the painted surface.

refs.name: Spider-Verse × Paper Hybrid
refs.use: Comic book physics, paper world construction, Miles-verse aesthetic, graphic story identity
refs.avoid: Actual Marvel IP, violent Spider-Man content
refs.dna: Spider-Verse paper world: environments constructed from visible layers of cut paper — background a painted landscape, midground a separate paper cutout layer, character on a top paper-flat cutout layer. The parallax of these layers is visible in camera movement. Comic-book halftone dots on the background layer. Characters cast paper-shadow onto the layer behind them. The world IS a comic book, assembled from its physical components.

refs.name: Ghibli × Felt Hybrid
refs.use: Handmade warmth, Ghibli characters, craft-world aesthetic, childhood-toy atmosphere
refs.dna: Studio Ghibli's textile warmth applied to felt-craft material: Ghibli's characteristic organic line and natural color palette, but every surface reads as handmade felt — animals have the fuzzy texture of embroidered wool, environments have the slightly rough surface of needle-felted landscape. The imperfection of craft IS the warmth of the world. Key light is gentle and diffused — the kind that shows felt texture without hard shadow.

refs.name: Anime × Chalk Hybrid
refs.use: Educational content in anime style, classroom teaching moments, subject concept visualization
refs.avoid: Copying specific anime character chalk drawings, too much violence energy
refs.dna: Anime visual language on blackboard chalk surface: dramatic shonen or shojo staging with the specific texture of chalk on dark board — linework is slightly rough at the edges, fills have the slight skip of chalk on texture, highlights are white chalk marks. The blackboard is the canvas; the anime grammar is the content. Bold chalk outlines, flat chalk-pigment color fills, smudge shadows. The whole frame could be erased with one hand.

refs.name: Pixar × Watercolor Hybrid
refs.use: Hybrid animation-watercolor, educational wonder, Pixar warmth meets paper texture, gentle teaching
refs.avoid: Copying Pixar IP characters, photo-realistic rendering — keep painterly and warm
refs.dna: Pixar aesthetic hybridized with watercolor paper texture: 3D character forms retain their dimensional warmth and subsurface scatter, but the backgrounds dissolve into hand-applied watercolor washes — the edges of objects bleed slightly into the painted ground, bridging digital and analog. Characters are fully rendered; environments are impressionist. The educational content appears as infographic elements that feel part of the watercolor world — diagrams drawn in the same palette as the background wash, labels in a handwritten font. The visual contract: the warmth of Pixar characters delivers trust; the watercolor paper delivers the intimacy of a teacher's notebook.

refs.name: Stop-Motion × Wood Hybrid
refs.use: Mechanical processes, how-things-work, construction sequences, physics and engineering for children
refs.avoid: Digital smooth animation feel — the handmade stutter and wood grain must be visible
refs.dna: Laika-grade stop-motion puppet weight built from carved and turned wood — each pivot joint clearly shows the grain and varnish catching the key light, camera close enough to see the wood texture and jointed mechanism, deliberate one-frame-at-a-time settle after each move, slight motion blur on action peak, stable final frame with the mechanism fully extended

refs.name: Kurzgesagt × Sand Hybrid
refs.use: Kurzgesagt geometry meets sand texture, cosmic scale with earthen warmth, explainer hybrid
refs.avoid: Rough or unclear sand forms — sand silhouettes must read as clean geometric Kurzgesagt shapes
refs.dna: Kurzgesagt's flat geometric system hybridized with sand-grain texture: the signature bird mascots and arrow-systems of Kurzgesagt information design are drawn as if on fine sand — each flat color field carries a granular texture visible at close range, the diagram lines have the slight imprecision of a finger-drawn line. The information hierarchy remains precise (Kurzgesagt never sacrifices clarity), but the texture communicates warmth and handcraft. Palette: Kurzgesagt's teal-to-orange gradient wheel maintained, but with sand's warm undertone softening the digital precision. Scale diagrams: the universe explained on a surface that feels like a desert floor.

refs.name: Whiteboard × Ink-Brush Hybrid
refs.use: Step-by-step processes, cause-and-effect chains, how-to sequences, lesson walkthroughs
refs.avoid: Messy or illegible strokes — every ink line must read clearly against the white surface
refs.dna: RSA-Animate whiteboard draw-on grammar with East Asian sumi-e ink-brush quality — single-stroke calligraphic weight as the drawing hand traces each concept element in sequence, white negative space dominates the frame, selective accent-color marker wash floods behind the key concept node, camera wide and locked as the diagram builds from left to right across the board

refs.name: Ghibli × Watercolor Hybrid
refs.use: Seasons, environment, cultural traditions, gentle emotional arcs, early childhood empathy content
refs.avoid: Copying Ghibli IP characters or specific Ghibli scenes
refs.dna: Ghibli diffused organic side-window key light applied to academic watercolor technique — wet-on-wet wash bleeding freely at sky and horizon edges, visible granulation in shadow fields, white paper highlights preserved on the primary concept subject, no hard outline on any edge, all boundaries softly bleeding into neighboring wash fields, supremely gentle temperature bias

refs.name: Anime × Wax-Crayon Hybrid
refs.use: Anime style with wax crayon texture, child-friendly energy, educational anime, classroom warmth
refs.avoid: Too much detail — wax crayon honesty requires bold, simple forms only
refs.dna: Anime character design hybridized with wax crayon texture: simplified anime-style characters (large eyes, clear emotional expression, dynamic poses) rendered with the visible wax stroke of a crayon — each color area shows the stroke direction, the slight translucency of wax over wax. Background environments are loose crayon-sketch, giving the educational content a spontaneous energy. The anime character at center functions as a guide figure: pointing, explaining, reacting with exaggerated emotion to the educational reveal. The educational content is embedded in the action — the science happens to the character, not beside them. Palette: bright crayon primaries, the kind available in the 24-count box.

refs.name: Three-Point Beauty Studio
refs.use: Three-point studio lighting, key-fill-rim setup, professional portrait, controlled quality light
refs.avoid: on-camera flash, muddy shadows, plastic skin
refs.dna: Three-point studio lighting grammar: key from the upper left at 45 degrees, source established (window or softbox), delivering the primary illumination that shapes the subject. Fill from the right at reduced intensity (1/4 to 1/2 stop below key), eliminating harsh shadow without eliminating shadow. Rim/hair light from behind at 3/4 angle, separating the subject from the background by illuminating the edge. Result: the subject is fully dimensional, the shadow side reveals depth, the rim confirms their presence in three-dimensional space. Background: continuous neutral or graduated. The professional portrait standard — not glamorous, not dramatic, but correct. Emotional temperature follows palette choice.

refs.name: Window-Light Testimonial
refs.use: Natural window light, directional daylight, architectural portrait, photographer grammar
refs.avoid: beauty smoothing, fake bokeh, fake smile
refs.dna: Window light grammar: natural directional light entering from a single source, usually north-facing for even quality or at an angle for Rembrandt-style drama. The window frames the light; the depth of the room establishes how quickly it falls off. Subject placement: 1 meter from the window for direct illumination, 2-3 meters for softer wrap. Reflector or white wall opposite: optional fill at reduced ratio. The hour matters: morning window is warm amber, midday window is spectrally neutral, afternoon window is golden. The curtain: if present, diffuses and wraps. The visual argument of window light: the most natural illumination is the one that falls from where light has always fallen.

refs.name: Product Macro Tabletop
refs.use: Product table, macro studio, ingredient beauty, tabletop commercial, still-life light
refs.avoid: plastic CGI sheen, warped logo, extra props
refs.dna: Tabletop studio grammar: the shooting surface as controlled environment. Table: white seamless, natural wood, slate, or marble — each material reads its temperature into the frame. Light from above at 45 degrees (standard for product) or sidelight at 10 degrees (for texture in food/ingredient shots). The object placement triangle: hero object, support element, texture element — three points of visual interest, one dominant. Background: seamless falling off to neutral gradient, or a wall establishing the environment. Practical element: a single motivating light source visible in frame (candle, small lamp) establishing the logical light origin. No shadow is accidental: every shadow is placed or removed with purpose.

refs.name: Handheld Verite Doc
refs.use: Documentary verité, handheld human, available-light truth, observational cinema
refs.avoid: stabilized gloss, stock smiling, staged crowd
refs.dna: Verité documentary grammar: the camera is a witness, not a director. Handheld at shoulder height, following rather than anticipating. Available light only: whatever exists in the space is the light. The frame finds truth by accepting imperfection — slight motion blur, brief exposure correction, the edge of the iris visible as it adjusts. The zoom is the verité tool: a slow pull-in to emphasis, fast pull-out for context, never the committed crane move of fiction cinematography. The interview happens at the location of the subject's daily life, not in a neutral studio. The mic appears in frame occasionally. The camera knows this because it is present, not because it is positioned.

refs.name: Golden-Hour Automotive
refs.use: Golden hour automotive, vehicle hero light, tarmac reflection, late sun drama
refs.avoid: HDR halo, fake lens flare, CGI gloss
refs.dna: Golden hour automotive grammar: the vehicle at 30 minutes before sunset, the sun at 5-10 degrees above the horizon. The bodywork reflects the sky's warm amber-orange at maximum saturation. The tarmac reflects the vehicle's shadow in long direction. Camera: low, at the vehicle's door-height, the sky visible behind the roofline. The vehicle is moving: motion blur at the wheels, a pan-follow shot that holds the vehicle in frame while the background streaks. The practical sequence: the window catches the sun for one second — the interior momentarily gold. Hold. The visual argument: a vehicle at this hour is as beautiful as anything that moves.

refs.name: High-Key Fashion Studio
refs.use: High-key brightness, low contrast, positive emotion, medical/corporate clean, bright neutral
refs.dna: High-key lighting grammar: the ratio between key and fill compressed to near-zero, the background brought to equal or near-equal brightness to the subject. The result is minimal shadow, maximum fill. Not 'overexposed' — correctly exposed so that the darker tones in the scene are already bright. Used for: positive emotion in corporate context, medical/pharmaceutical reassurance, beauty/skincare softness, e-commerce product neutrality. The visual emotion is optimistic, open, undramatic. Key source: large softbox from above-front. Fill: large reflector or second softbox. Background: white seamless or near-white. The subject exists in light, not against it.

refs.name: Low-Key Chiaroscuro Portrait
refs.use: Low-key drama, deep shadow, single motivated key, chiaroscuro, noir intimacy
refs.avoid: flat fill, crushed black, beauty smoothing
refs.dna: Low-key lighting grammar: a single motivated key source, everything else in shadow. The key source is justified — a desk lamp, a streetlight visible through a window, a candle — the motivation makes the shadow credible. Shadow fills 60-80% of the frame. The subject is revealed by the key, confirmed by the rim (if present), and isolated by the darkness behind them. Palette follows the key's color temperature: tungsten reads amber and intimate, fluorescent reads clinical and anxious, moonlight reads cold and solitary. The visual emotion is determined by what remains visible: a face half in shadow says something different than hands emerging from darkness.

refs.name: Practical Neon Night Street
refs.use: Urban neon practical, wet street reflection, night city ambiance, commercial night grammar
refs.avoid: uniform neon wash, CGI haze, flat fog
refs.dna: Neon street night grammar: the subject lit by existing urban practicals — neon signage (pharmacy cross, restaurant name, hotel identity) at 3-5 meters distance, casting colored light in the 2700-3200K range. The street is wet: each practical source reflects in the pavement, doubling the light sources. The camera: wide lens at f/1.4 or f/2, the neon background bokeh circles of color. The frame contains at least 3 distinct neon colors simultaneously — the mix creates the urban night palette. The subject stands at the point where two neon sources create a color split: half in warm (red/orange), half in cool (blue/green). Rain optional but preferred.

refs.name: Overcast Soft Daylight
refs.use: Overcast diffuse, natural softbox, outdoor portraiture, even light, outdoor documentary
refs.avoid: flat boredom without a contrast accent
refs.dna: Overcast sky grammar: the entire sky as a single diffuse source, 180 degrees overhead. The result is directionless fill — no hard shadow exists, every surface receives equal illumination from above. The visual character: clean skin rendering, maximum color saturation in the environment (no specular highlights competing with material color), the world existing in its own color without light-drama. Best for: outdoor portrait where expression matters over drama, documentary where environment must read fully, product where material color must be accurate. The color temperature is spectrally neutral (5500-6500K daylight). A reflector pointed at the subject's face adds the one directional note.

refs.name: Hard Sun Desert Commercial
refs.use: Midday hard sun, desert environment, harsh shadow, high-contrast documentary, stamina
refs.dna: Hard sun desert grammar: the sun at zenith, no diffusion, the hardest possible light from directly above. Shadows are black and short. The subject's face is lit from above — in portrait terms, raccoon shadows under the eyes, the chin in shadow. This is correct for content about stamina, endurance, survival, desert environment, midday travel. The sky: white-blue, no cloud. The horizon: heat shimmer at 200 meters. The subject squints; this is not a mistake but proof of the environment. Palette: bleached sand ochre, sky blue-white, the subject's skin at maximum heat. The challenge: everything correct about this lighting is the opposite of flattering. That is the point.

refs.name: Tungsten Interview Warmth
refs.use: Tungsten practical warmth, interior amber, hotel intimacy, residential evening, warm artificial
refs.avoid: orange cast overload, fake bokeh balls
refs.dna: Tungsten practical grammar: interior illumination from existing tungsten sources — Edison bulbs, hotel bedside lamps, restaurant pendants at 2700K. The world shifts amber: the shadows have orange cast, the walls warm, skin has a golden temperature. White-balance choice is the creative decision: correct to neutral and lose the warmth, leave at tungsten white balance and the world is amber. The frame: a reading lamp creating a pool of light on a desk, everything beyond falling off to warm darkness. The visual emotion: intimacy, domesticity, evening time, the end of the day. The light is not trying to reveal — it is trying to comfort.

refs.name: Anamorphic Wide Landscape
refs.use: Anamorphic lens signature, horizontal flare, oval bokeh, cinematic grammar, premium look
refs.dna: Anamorphic lens grammar: the horizontal lens flare as the diagnostic — when a practical source appears in the frame, blue-white light streaks horizontally across the full width. The bokeh is oval, not circular — vertical ovals in the out-of-focus background communicate the 2.39:1 format. The image is slightly darker at the edges (vignette) without software correction. The depth of field is long but selective. These artifacts are not corrected because they communicate cinema: the anamorphic lens was invented for Cinemascope and carries 70 years of theatrical viewing in its optical signature. The visual argument: this was shot on glass that was designed for a theater screen.

refs.name: Shonen Adventure Narrative Grammar
refs.use: Adventure beginnings, world reveals, character introduction, journey start, inspiration
refs.avoid: protected characters, pirate logos, exact ship/world copy
refs.dna: The establishing shot of a shonen adventure: a wide frame showing a vast world beyond the protagonist's starting point — a road leading to distant mountains, a river flowing to the sea, a horizon with something massive and unknown at its edge. The protagonist is in the lower-left, looking toward the right (the direction of narrative progress). Warm light. Big sky. The world is larger than this person, and that is the invitation. No villain visible. No obstacle named. Only direction.

refs.name: Soul Pressure Reveal Grammar
refs.use: Power reveals, authority hierarchies, spiritual pressure, boss introductions, transcendence moments
refs.avoid: direct character, sword, uniform, logo or world copy
refs.dna: Spiritual hierarchy made physical: a figure radiating an aura so powerful that the environment responds — other figures are forced to their knees or pushed backward, the ground cracks beneath the source, the air distorts and darkens around the radius of influence. The aura has a specific color tied to its nature. The powerful figure is perfectly still at center; everything else deforms around that stillness. Scale of aura = scale of power.

refs.name: Premium Product Desire Grammar
refs.use: Product desire narrative, object reveal, material surface proof, premium desire, geometry beauty
refs.avoid: trailer energy, fake luxury, education cues
refs.dna: The product desire narrative grammar: a single object introduced at the moment of maximum desire — not in use, not explained, but displayed at frame center on a surface that reads its material quality back to the viewer. Marble: cold and ancient. Fabric: warm and yielding. Glass: honest and unforgiving. One motivated light source traverses the object's surface, revealing geometry in 10 seconds of slow dolly-in — waist distance to detail-close. No human hands. No context. No copy or call-to-action. Just the object and the visual argument of what it promises. Hold on the detail shot 3 seconds after the motion completes. The viewer must want it before the frame ends.

refs.name: Civic Child-Height Documentary Grammar
refs.use: Civic documentary, institutional proof, child scale, democratic participation, public service film
refs.avoid: stock civic montage, drone spectacle, staged smiles
refs.dna: The civic documentary narrative grammar: the institutional action — ballot cast, permit stamped, crossing guard signal given — filmed at child height. Camera at 90 centimeters from the ground, the adult world towering above and around. One proof action (a raised hand, a green light, a stamp pressed) makes the machinery of civic life visible and human. The shot holds 3 seconds after the action completes: long enough for the weight of the civic moment to land. No music. Natural sound only. The institution is only legible at the scale of the individual it serves. The visual argument: democracy is not architecture, it is the gesture of the person inside the architecture.

refs.name: Golden Hour Motivated Warmth
refs.use: long warm amber key low on the horizon, soft wrap, gentle haze, premium glow
refs.avoid: flat midday flatness, cold white balance
refs.dna: motivated low warm key, long amber shadows, soft falloff, gentle bloom, skin-true warmth

refs.name: Anamorphic Epic Scale
refs.use: wide anamorphic field, horizontal flares, atmosphere and light shafts, deep staging, sense of scale
refs.dna: anamorphic width, oval bokeh, horizontal streak flares, haze depth cues, epic scale staging

refs.name: Noir High-Contrast Night
refs.use: hard single key, deep crushed shadow, practical pools, wet specular
refs.dna: high-contrast chiaroscuro, hard key, deep shadow, selective practical light, moody falloff

refs.name: Teal-Orange Commercial Balance
refs.use: warm skin against cool environment, clean studio control, glossy product light
refs.dna: teal shadow / warm key balance, controlled gloss, clean separation, premium commercial polish

refs.name: High-Key Clean Studio
refs.use: bright even soft light, low contrast, airy white set, gentle shadow
refs.dna: high-key soft wrap, low contrast, bright clean field, minimal shadow, fresh airy mood

refs.name: Overcast Documentary Naturalism
refs.use: soft diffused daylight, true colour, observational realism, available light
refs.dna: soft overcast key, even diffusion, naturalistic true colour, restrained handheld honesty

refs.name: Neon Night Practical Pools
refs.use: saturated neon practicals, wet reflective street, colored pools of light
refs.dna: saturated neon practicals, magenta/cyan pools, wet specular reflections, moody night depth

refs.name: Hard-Key Drama
refs.use: single hard source, sharp shadow edge, strong modeling, tense mood
refs.dna: hard directional key, crisp shadow edge, strong facial/object modeling, dramatic contrast

refs.name: Soft Window Light
refs.use: large soft side window source, gentle gradient falloff, intimate calm
refs.dna: soft motivated window key from one side, gentle wrap, quiet intimate gradient

refs.name: Backlit Silhouette
refs.use: strong backlight rim, subject as shape, atmospheric separation
refs.dna: motivated backlight rim, silhouette read, haze separation, glowing edge

refs.name: Handheld Vérité Energy
refs.use: loose handheld frame, reactive reframing, real-moment immediacy
refs.dna: handheld micro-movement, reactive reframe, documentary immediacy, present-tense energy

refs.name: Symmetrical Formal Frame
refs.use: centered symmetry, controlled formal composition, deliberate stillness
refs.dna: centered symmetrical composition, formal balance, deliberate static control, graphic order

refs.name: Macro Surface Intimacy
refs.use: extreme macro, shallow focus, tactile surface truth, micro detail
refs.dna: macro proximity, shallow focus plane, tactile surface micro-detail, intimate scale

refs.name: Deep-Focus Landscape Scale
refs.use: deep focus, foreground-to-horizon clarity, grand natural scale
refs.dna: deep focus front-to-back, layered depth, grand landscape scale, clear atmospheric perspective

refs.name: Motivated Naturalistic Key
refs.use: believable in-world sources only, restrained naturalism, honest contrast
refs.dna: naturalistic motivated key (light logic only, never a copy), source-honest contrast, restrained elegance

refs.name: Available-Light Realism
refs.use: existing practical light, low intervention, candid truth
refs.dna: available-light realism, practical-only sources, candid low-intervention honesty

refs.name: Hero Academia-like Burst Energy Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: MHA peak effort, hero combat, Bones animation prestige, quirk visual grammar
refs.avoid: protected hero costumes, emblems, school logos, exact character likeness or frame copy
refs.dna: Bones' My Hero Academia combat grammar: the quirk activation as the most specific visual moment. Midoriya's Delaware Smash: the punch impact frame holds for 3 additional frames, energy radiating outward as concentric rings while the target's position shifts in slow motion before impact physics take over. Todoroki's left-right divide: the literal split of ice-blue and fire-orange down the center of his body, the line between them never negotiating. All Might's size: in hero form his figure is simply larger than it should be, and the animation never acknowledges this as unusual. The Bones trademark: in the peak moment, everything else in the frame simplifies to a colored blur behind the one thing that matters.

refs.name: Hunter-like Tactical Aura Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Hunter x Hunter Nen system, Togashi visual theory, strategic energy, psychological combat
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, license-card props, exact ability designs or frame copy
refs.dna: Madhouse's Hunter x Hunter grammar: the Nen system as psychological grammar. The aura is not spectacular display but concentrated intent — the visual representation of focused will. Gon's aura in Jajanken: a tightly contained black energy at the fist, implying contained violence more threatening than the explosion. The strategic layering: on-screen text or narrator explaining the tactical situation, the image and the explanation working together. Killua's godspeed: the lightning is in his eyes before it is in his hands. Gon-san transformation: the camera stays at a respectful distance, acknowledging the moment's weight by not dramatizing it. The visual grammar of a manga-ka who trusts the reader's intelligence.

refs.name: Vinland-like North Epic Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Viking saga, Norse age, Wit Studio animation, Thorfinn journey, historical anime
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, exact ship ornament, sigils, frame copy
refs.dna: Wit Studio's Vinland Saga grammar: the Viking Age rendered with the weight of actual historical consequence. Ships at sea in grey North Atlantic light, the horizon a flat line distinguishing grey water from grey sky. Battle scenes with the weariness of men who have been fighting too long: the choreography is economical, not spectacular. Thorfinn's expressionless face: a design choice — a child who has witnessed too much, the emotion compressed into posture and the angle of the chin. Palette: Norse grey-green-brown, the specific cold of Northern European winter light, blood as a color that means something, not as decoration. The visual register: this is history. These things happened.

refs.name: Mob-like Psychic Wave Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: ONE/Bones supernatural psychic, Shigeo Kageyama, psychic wave energy, emotional explosion
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, exact aura designs, logos, frame copy
refs.dna: Bones' Mob Psycho 100 grammar: the suppressed emotion as visual pressure. Mob's percentage counter approaching 100%: the environment begins to fail — glass breaks spontaneously, objects orbit the figure, wind from nowhere. The release: the counter hits 100% (or ???%) and the psychic energy is not explosion but implosion-then-expansion — first everything is pulled toward Mob, then released outward in a wave that the animation renders at 24 drawings per second of maximum visual commitment. The color system: normal Mob is underdrawn, deliberately plain — ONE's manga style preserved. Psychic Mob: the world around him becomes a Yusuke Murata-level spectacle. The contrast is the argument.

refs.name: Berserk-like Dark Engraving Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Kentaro Miura dark fantasy, Eclipse horror, Griffin tragedy, manga engraving grammar
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, brand marks, exact armor/sword design, frame copy
refs.dna: Kentaro Miura's Berserk grammar: the most detailed line art in manga history applied to the darkest possible content. The demon apostle bodies: designed as corruptions of the human form — familiar enough to feel the wrongness, grotesque enough to communicate the violation. The Eclipse: the crimson moon, the void, the brand of sacrifice. The Dragon Slayer sword: so large it breaks physics, its size the visual representation of rage. Guts' silhouette: always in motion, never at rest, the figure that will not stop despite everything. The palette of the anime adaptations: dark amber-gold against black void. The visual argument: there is beauty in persistence, even when persistence is all that remains.

refs.name: Monster-like Quiet Dread Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Naoki Urasawa suspense, European crime drama, quiet horror, detective melancholy
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, real-institution logos, exact frame copy
refs.dna: Naoki Urasawa's Monster grammar: the horror of normalcy. Dr. Tenma in European streets — post-reunification Germany, Prague, Düsseldorf — cities that look ordinary and feel menacing because something wrong has happened in them. Johan Liebert: the most beautiful face in any room, the stillness around him that precedes death. Urasawa's visual technique: long establishing shots of environments before any character appears, the geography establishing the emotional register. Palette: muted European autumn — grey stone, leaf-brown, the amber of indoor light against cold blue exterior. The horror is not in what is shown but in what each character's expression implies about what happened just before this frame.

refs.name: JoJo-like Pose Graphic Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Hirohiko Araki pose grammar, JoJo stance, graphic novel panel, fashion-influenced combat
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, stand designs, logos, exact panel copy
refs.dna: Hirohiko Araki's JoJo's Bizarre Adventure grammar: the pose as the primary visual unit. Not the action that precedes or follows, but the held pose — the stance that communicates who this person is at their core, drawn with the anatomical confidence of someone who has studied both Renaissance sculpture and Versace runway photography. The Araki palette: each JoJo era has its own color scheme (Phantom Blood earth-tones, Diamond is Unbreakable pastel pop, Golden Wind Italian oil-painting). The Stand reveal: the Stand and user photographed together, the Stand's design always reflecting its user's personality through visual rhyme. The panel border itself bends when the content demands it.

refs.name: One-Punch-like Contrast Comedy Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: One Punch Man contrast, Saitama invincibility, ONE underdrawing, Madhouse spectacle contrast
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, hero-association marks, exact frame copy
refs.dna: Madhouse's One Punch Man visual grammar: the most important contrast in modern anime. Villains rendered at maximum visual spectacle — elaborate character designs, transformation sequences at theatrical scale, particle effects at maximum density. Saitama: a pale, round-headed figure in a yellow jumpsuit, drawn deliberately simply. When the one punch lands: all that spectacle is replaced by one motion across the frame and a crater. The visual argument is structural: all the world's visual complexity and threat resolves into a single ordinary gesture. The comedy and the tragedy occupy the same frame because the contrast is simultaneously funny and sad.

refs.name: Violet-like Light Elegance Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: KyoAni emotional elegance, Violet Evergarden grammar, letter-writing, post-war melancholy
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, uniform/brooch design, exact frame copy
refs.dna: Kyoto Animation's Violet Evergarden grammar: the animation studio's commitment to background art as emotional landscape. The European fantasy continent in the golden age of the letter: Violet in her prosthetic hands typing on a keyboard that generates emotional consequence. The backgrounds are the most detailed in television anime: cobblestone streets photographed and painted over, the quality of light through curtains rendered correctly as diffuse directionality. Violet's blue eyes as the emotional register: they communicate what her expression cannot because she has not yet learned to express. Palette: the ochre of parchment, the blue of Violet's dress and eyes, the specific green of summer European fantasy countryside.

refs.name: Haikyuu-like Sport Momentum Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Sports anime volleyball, Haruichi Furudate grammar, motion line precision, team competition
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, team crests, exact frame copy
refs.dna: Production I.G's Haikyuu!! grammar: the volleyball in motion as the organizing principle of every combat frame. The spike trajectory line visible before and after contact — a curved path showing the ball's route. Speed lines not as generic radials but as directional arcs following the ball's actual physics. The height: Hinata at 164cm visible against blockers at 190+cm, the height differential carrying the underdog narrative. Court perspective: wide-angle low establishing shot for the play's strategy, then fast-cut close-ups for the individual effort, return to wide for resolution. The sound and silence: the moment before serve is the loudest silence in sports animation.

refs.name: Vagabond-like Ink Brush Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Takehiko Inoue ink brush mastery, Miyamoto Musashi, swordsman realism, Japanese art manga
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, historical-figure portraits as identity, exact panel copy
refs.dna: Takehiko Inoue's Vagabond ink brush grammar: the finest application of sumi-e ink technique to modern manga. The brush line is not even — it thickens at the middle of a stroke, thins at the end, the variation communicating the hand's movement through the physical quality of the mark. The Musashi portrait: the face constructed from overlapping washes of diluted ink, the features emerging from value rather than outline. Action sequences: the brush stroke itself becomes the speed line, the ink wash the blur. The page composition: sometimes the illustration ignores the grid entirely, the image occupying the full spread as a single painting. The visual argument: manga can be high art without ceasing to be manga.

refs.name: Abyss-like Vertical Wonder Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Made in Abyss vertical descent, Tsukushi Akihito grammar, wonder and dread, layered world
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, creature copies, relic designs, frame copy
refs.dna: Kinema Citrus' Made in Abyss grammar: the Abyss itself as the visual protagonist. The descent: the camera's orientation is always vertical, looking down into depths that dissolve into warm cloud cover hiding the next layer. The layer transitions are biome-shifts: the first layer's manageable jungle becomes the second layer's inverted architecture becomes the fifth layer's ocean of corpses. The contradiction: the color palette is always beautiful — teal, gold, amber, warm cloud light — and the content is increasingly horrifying. Riko and Reg are small against the Abyss in every frame. The visual argument: wonder and dread are not opposites but the same feeling at different concentrations of comprehension.

refs.name: Nolan-like IMAX Practical Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Practical spectacle, real-world physics, war, space, historical drama, temporal confusion
refs.avoid: director name-dropping in output, studio logos, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Nolan's practical IMAX: a 70mm film frame of a real physical event — bomber over a real city, a figure in a practical spacesuit against a real IMAX sky backdrop, an analog detonation in a real desert. The grain of the celluloid is visible. Desaturated warm-brown palette with selective blue-grey for scale. No CGI tells — every edge has the physics of the real world. Time may be non-linear but the photography never knows it.

refs.name: Spielberg-like Backlight Wonder Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Wonder, encounter, childhood perspective, alien contact, miracle, suburban fantasy
refs.avoid: director name-dropping, franchise creatures, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Spielberg's Encounter light: a figure silhouetted in a doorway against an impossibly bright backlight — the source is so bright it creates no detail in the subject, only a dark shape inside a field of white-gold light. The viewer leans toward the light. Child eye-line: the camera is at 90cm height, making adults' hips the top of the frame. Lens flare from the backlight source is not a mistake, it is the message: something divine has arrived.

refs.name: Wong Kar-wai-like Neon Melancholy Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Memory, yearning, Hong Kong, slow cinema, missed connections, neon rain atmosphere
refs.avoid: director name-dropping, brand signage, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Wong Kar-wai's blurred yearning: a figure in motion through a neon-lit corridor or night market, shot at half-speed or with step-printing that breaks them into afterimage layers — multiple ghost positions of the same body moving through the same space, all slightly out of sync. Palette: saturated green-teal neon, red-amber street lamps, midnight blue. The frame is always about missing something — a person, a time, a city that no longer exists.

refs.name: Villeneuve-like Fog Monolith Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: First contact, impossible scale, sci-fi awe, fog/atmosphere as staging tool, slow revelation
refs.avoid: director name-dropping, alien/ship design copies, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Villeneuve's monolith arrival: an enormous object — alien ship, desert worm, industrial structure — emerges slowly from fog or sand at the bottom of the frame. Its scale only becomes clear when a tiny human figure is visible at the edge. The object moves at a speed too slow to register in real time. Palette: cold grey-blue fog, warm amber sand, the object is always dark (charcoal-black or deep brown-grey) against the pale diffused sky. Silence is mandatory.

refs.name: Malick-like Golden Poetry Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Spiritual introspection, guilt and grace, nature as divine, wide-open landscapes, poetic drama
refs.avoid: director name-dropping, voiceover imitation as content, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Malick's golden-hour internal monologue: a figure walking through a field of tall grass at magic hour, the camera circling at waist height — grass in the foreground in soft focus, figure intermittently visible, sky burning amber-gold behind. Voice-over would go here. No establishing shot, no exposition, no eye-lines that make sense geographically. The cinematography (Lubezki) makes the world feel remembered rather than present. It IS a memory, even if it hasn't happened yet.

refs.name: Hitchcock-like Suspense Geometry Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Suspense geometry, voyeurism, pursuit, architectural dread, The Wrong Man, MacGuffin logic
refs.avoid: director name-dropping, film-title references, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Hitchcock's geometric anxiety: a staircase seen from directly above — the spiral or zigzag descent visible as pure graphic form, a figure descending it reduced to a shape moving through a pattern. Or: a long hotel corridor from ground level, two parallel walls, the subject at the far end making the destination visible but far. The camera holds on the geometry after the subject has left, because the geometry is still the problem. Palette: crisp black-and-white or Technicolor green-amber.

refs.name: Del Toro-like Gothic Fairy Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Dark fairy tale, creature beauty, childhood trauma, Francoist Spain, golden labyrinth
refs.avoid: director name-dropping, creature design copies, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Del Toro's tactile fairy horror: extreme close-up on a creature or object with practical effects — organic texture, eyeballs in unexpected locations, bioluminescent patches on insect-like limbs, gold-leaf filigree on a monstrous surface. The monster is beautiful. Interior of the old house: warm amber practical light, deep shadow, books and insects and clocks. Pale child against the dark world. Palette: deep red, amber-gold, verdigris green, pale cream, absolute black.

refs.name: Tarantino-like Pulp Frame Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Crime comedy-drama, pop-culture dialogue, non-linear narrative, explosive violence in ordinary frame
refs.avoid: director name-dropping, violence glorification in output, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Tarantino's static pop shot: the camera is at table-top or dashboard level, looking slightly upward at a figure who is doing something mundane (eating, talking, driving) while something about the context is wrong. Long pauses in cutting — faces held at duration. Dialogue is the action. When violence comes, it is quick and without preparation. Palette: 1970s warm film stock (golden-orange, avocado, washed-out blue), or high-contrast red-white-black for the chapters.

refs.name: IKEA-like Clean Home Story Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Domestic warmth, family evidence, democratic design, real apartment, morning light
refs.avoid: brand logos/wordmarks, catalog page copies, competitor references
refs.dna: IKEA's democratic warmth grammar: a real apartment at 8am, natural window light from the left, a dining table holding evidence of a family — a child's drawing pinned to the wall, half-finished coffee, a chair pulled at an angle. The room is not staged for beauty but for recognition. Lens: 35mm at eye level, entering through a threshold. Palette: the room's own — IKEA's signature is never color but a quality of unpretentious warmth. No perfect symmetry. The subject is always a person; the product is always incidental to the life being lived around it.

refs.name: Airline-like Destination Scale Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Destination film, airline scale, international travel, golden-hour tarmac, scale vs. sky
refs.avoid: airline logos/liveries, real airport branding, exact ad copy
refs.dna: Turkish Airlines destination grammar: the airport tarmac at golden hour, aircraft surfaces reflecting the last warm amber light. A single traveler silhouette against overwhelming scale — the aircraft is enormous, the human figure small, the destination implied but not shown. Wide-angle perspective lines find the terminal gate, the runway, the horizon. The visual argument: to board is to exist temporarily at a different altitude. Palette: Turkish red accent against each destination's native palette — Venice terracotta, Bali green, Tokyo pewter. The product is scale and warmth, not a seat. Hero framing elevates the ordinary departure into a promise.

refs.name: Energy-like Action POV Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Extreme sport, first-person action, gravity defiance, adrenaline, outdoor velocity
refs.avoid: energy-drink logos, athlete identities, exact stunt copies
refs.dna: Red Bull's first-person extreme grammar: the camera is the helmet, the board, the wing — body is the rig. The athlete's gloved hands appear at frame edges reaching toward the next hold, rail, or wave. Horizon tilts past 45 degrees, communicating committed speed. Real-world 4K texture — trail dust, ocean spray, snow crystal blur — as atmospheric proof of physical commitment. Gravity is both antagonist and medium. The world is a vertical landscape. The frame says: some humans have chosen to live at this angle to the earth, and the camera has gone with them.

refs.name: Couture-like Monochrome Luxury Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: High fashion, monochrome elegance, luxury restraint, couture structure, brand silence
refs.avoid: fashion-house logos, designer names, exact campaign copy
refs.dna: Chanel's monochrome grammar (Lagerfeld era): absolute black and white, no grey zone, no middle tone allowed. One hard key from directly above or from a precise 45-degree angle carves the structure of a face or garment in pure value contrast. Silk catches light differently than skin — the camera demonstrates this. Negative space is not empty but active, framing the subject as sculpture. Color is a distraction from form. The brand's argument: luxury is not decoration, it is architecture. The frame holds its silence the way a good suit fits — with zero visible effort.

refs.name: Brick-like Modular Build Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Creative play, modular construction, optimistic building, childhood scale, assembly joy
refs.avoid: toy-brand logos, minifigure face copies, set-box artwork
refs.dna: LEGO's visual grammar of creation: bricks in mid-assembly, frozen at the instant before the last piece clicks into place. Primary colors at full saturation — LEGO yellow, red, blue — against a clean white surface. Camera at brick level, finding the architectural logic of 2x4 studs. Scale trick: a minifigure stands next to an enormous in-progress construction, reversing the adult/child size relationship. The 2x4 stud pattern is the signature — no logo required. The visual argument: anything can be unmade and remade. Pure optimism in ABS plastic. No cynicism exists in this frame.

refs.name: Marque-like Silver Precision Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Luxury automotive, precision engineering, studio reveal, bodywork sculpture, marque authority
refs.avoid: car-brand logos/badges, exact model design, slogan copies
refs.dna: Mercedes-Benz studio grammar: the vehicle in three-quarter studio light, two key sources creating the light-flow signature over silver bodywork — one specular traveling the hood, one defining the wheel arch geometry. Camera at vehicle eye-height, never above. Chrome elements multiply reflections deliberately. Background: darkened cyclorama or minimal grey gradient — no environment competes. The visual argument is engineering mastery, not speed or comfort. The logo appears only in specular reflection, earned rather than placed. The car is complete without context; context would diminish it.

refs.name: Stream-like Duotone Pop Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Music streaming, duotone graphic, performer silhouette, genre poster, cultural moment
refs.avoid: streaming-brand logos/UI copies, artist likeness, exact campaign copy
refs.dna: Spotify's duotone grammar: two colors, maximum graphic contrast, subject reduced to pure silhouette form — performer, instrument, dancing body — as flat shape against a solid field. One color is always dark (navy, deep purple, black), one is the accent (electric green, coral, warm yellow). No shadow gradients, no texture, no photography grain — the image reads in 0.5 seconds at thumbnail size. The grammar of the streaming era: infinite scroll, one frame to stop you. When the silhouette pulses once with the beat, that is the only motion allowed. Music as graphic poster, not photograph.

refs.name: Sekiro-like Ink Resolve Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: FromSoftware precise combat, autumn Japan, ink-brush motion, stillness before violence
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, studio marks, exact boss/scene copy
refs.dna: FromSoftware's Sekiro grammar: a shinobi in stillness — the pause before perfect execution. Autumn maple leaves fall slowly past the frame, red against grey stone. The posture communicates absolute readiness through near-total immobility; only the leaves move. Ink-brush aesthetic applied to action: when movement comes it is a single committed stroke, not hesitation. Blood-petal confirmation: after the cut, a single petal touches the blade. Palette: autumn red, charcoal grey, muted gold lacquer, white snow (Hirata Estate), black void. The visual grammar of the perfect parry: stillness, the instant of contact, consequence. Nothing is wasted.

refs.name: Witcher-like Dark Folk Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Slavic dark fantasy, grey moral choices, fairy tale realism, mud and candlelight
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, medallion/armor design copies, exact scene copy
refs.dna: CD Projekt Red's Witcher grammar: the fairy tales turned into a grey moral problem. A Witcher approaching a village at dusk — the village is lit by candle and hearth light, the surrounding forest is dark and full.
