The Witcher's medallion catches the light as the only clean thing in the frame. Folk costume with real mud, real wear, real texture — not fantasy nobility but the working poor of a continent at war. Palette: earthy brown-ochre, hearth amber, forest shadow green-black, the silver of the medallion and the sword. The moral register: everyone is partially wrong, everyone is partially right, and the job must be done.

refs.name: Skyrim-like Nordic Frost Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Nordic tundra, ancient stone, aurora sky, Dragonborn scale, open world grandeur
refs.avoid: protected creature/armor designs, studio marks, exact scene copy
refs.dna: Bethesda's Skyrim grammar: the view from a mountain pass at altitude — the entire province spread below, snow-capped peaks at the horizon, a Dwemer ruin visible at mid-distance. Aurora borealis filling the upper 40% of the frame with green-white light ribbons. The Dragonborn stands at the foreground edge of a cliff, scale established by the landscape's enormity. Nordic carved stone in the foreground — runic inscriptions, weathered and partially snow-covered. Palette: snow-white, aurora green, deep Nordic sky blue, stone grey, warm amber torchlight at the ruin's entrance below. The visual argument: the world is enormous, and you are invited to cross every horizon.

refs.name: Plumber-like Color Platform Joy Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Platform joy, bright primary colors, bounce physics, cartoon happiness, Miyamoto optimism
refs.avoid: protected mascot likeness, logos, exact level/asset copy
refs.dna: Miyamoto's Super Mario grammar: a world built for bounce physics — the jump arc is deliberate, readable, joyful. Primary palette at absolute maximum: Mario red, sky blue, Mushroom Kingdom green, coin gold. The environment is toy-scale with human character — pipes are larger than they should be, ? blocks float at eye level. Camera: 2.5D perspective establishing both lateral movement and depth. The coins create a trail that reads as an invitation — follow the shiny path. The Goomba mushroom face communicates existential sadness through simplicity. No darkness: this world's enemies are not dangerous but incorrectly placed.

refs.name: Sonic-like Speed Loop Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Speed personality, loop-de-loop geometry, blue motion blur, SEGA momentum
refs.avoid: protected mascot likeness, logos, exact zone/asset copy
refs.dna: SEGA's Sonic grammar: velocity as character trait. The camera struggles to keep pace — motion blur edges the frame in electric blue-gold. The loop-de-loop is the signature architecture: a perfectly circular tube that violates human intuition (you should fall) but Sonic navigates effortlessly. Speed lines radiate horizontally from the center. The environment is rendered in vector-clean backgrounds: Green Hill Zone's rolling planes and palm trees, Chemical Plant's industrial geometry. Palette: Sonic blue against opposite colors — green grass, industrial grey, casino gold. The visual argument: speed has a personality, and it is blue and impatient.

refs.name: Fighter-like Impact Frame Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Fighting game impact, single contact frame, brush-stroke energy, Capcom action grammar
refs.avoid: protected fighter likeness, logos, exact stage/move copy
refs.dna: Capcom's Street Fighter grammar: the single impact frame — the 1/60th second of maximum contact between two fighters, frozen as a composition. Energy bursts radiate outward from the point of contact as brush-stroke speed lines. The impact is always center-frame, both fighters visible in the same locked composition. Character design at maximum silhouette readability: Ryu's headband and gi, Chun-Li's buns, Guile's flat-top — each readable in peripheral vision. The energy effect (Hadouken fireball, Kikōken charge) uses its character's palette signature. Background simplifies during super moves to pure energy color. The frame holds 2 seconds after impact.

refs.name: Ottoman Miniature Painting
refs.use: Ottoman illuminated manuscript, Nakkaşhane workshop grammar, elevated perspective, Topkapı art
refs.avoid: Western perspective depth, photorealism, shadow casting, modern style
refs.dna: Ottoman Nakkaşhane miniature grammar (16th-17th century): the elevated perspective that sees everything without vanishing point — figures in the lower register, architecture in middle-ground, landscape in background, all visible at once in the flat plane. The border is as important as the interior: gold-leaf arabesque patterning, lapis blue cartouche headers, crimson divider lines. Human figures in profile, always in motion, never in direct eye contact with the viewer — the court painter's convention of respectful distance. The palette is jewel-tone against white paper: lapis lazuli blue, vermillion red, malachite green, gold leaf. The visual grammar of an empire that saw the world from the perspective of God looking down.

refs.name: Hokusai — Ukiyo-e Woodblock
refs.use: Ukiyo-e woodblock, Hokusai wave grammar, Japanese nature power, The Great Wave register
refs.avoid: photorealism, Western shadow, 3D depth, gradient fills
refs.dna: Katsushika Hokusai's woodblock grammar: the natural world at its most formally perfect. The Great Wave composition — the wave at its peak forming a claw above three boats, Mount Fuji visible in the background as a small blue-white triangle of calm against the wave's enormous energy. The woodblock technique: line carving with gradated printing, the Prussian blue color theory (Prussian blue was recently imported, Hokusai used it with the excitement of a new discovery). No photographic shadow — the form is defined by line and color area. The ocean has an almost mathematical elegance in its threat. The wave is beautiful and lethal simultaneously, and Hokusai treats both qualities with equal precision.

refs.name: Bauhaus Geometric Design
refs.use: Bauhaus design grammar, primary forms, type as visual element, Moholy-Nagy grammar, total design
refs.avoid: organic curves, photorealism, decorative ornament, busy detail
refs.dna: Bauhaus design grammar (Dessau era, 1925-1932): the grid as the substrate. Three primary colors plus black, white, grey — no other colors allowed, because these are the colors from which all others are derived and to which all others reduce. Three primary forms: circle, square, triangle, each with a corresponding primary color (yellow triangle, red square, blue circle per Kandinsky's theory). Typography as visual element, not just information — Futura or its derivatives, type blocks as graphic mass. The visual argument of the Bauhaus: if it serves a function, it can be beautiful through the honesty of its function. Design is not decoration; decoration is the failure of design.

refs.name: Retro NASA Travel Poster
refs.use: Space tourism retro poster, WPA graphic style, celestial landscape, exoplanet advertising
refs.avoid: photorealistic space CGI, busy star fields, modern color grade
refs.dna: NASA's Visions of the Future poster series grammar: the WPA National Parks poster style applied to exoplanet tourism. Each poster presents a celestial body as a travel destination — HD 40307g as Super Earth with multiple moons, Kepler-16b as a binary-star sunset world. The style: flat color areas in earth-pigment palette (ochre, burnt sienna, forest green, sky blue), simplified human or robotic figure in foreground for scale, celestial vista in background. Typography in WPA slab-serif, the tourism tagline communicating scientific fact as invitation. The visual argument: space exploration is the greatest travel photography project ever undertaken, and every exoplanet is a destination.

refs.name: Dutch Golden Age — Still Life Light
refs.use: Vanitas still life, Vermeer window light, Dutch Golden Age, memento mori, material beauty
refs.avoid: flat lighting, modern environment, photographic grain, over-exposure
refs.dna: Dutch Golden Age still-life grammar (Jan Davidsz de Heem, Pieter Claesz era): the vanitas composition — objects arranged on a table cloth to communicate the pleasure and transience of material existence. A Nautilus shell (value and rarity), grapes (pleasure), a half-peeled lemon (bittersweet, and technically challenging to paint), an overturned glass (disorder approaching), flowers past their peak. The light source: a single north window at the upper left, not visible in frame but casting soft directional illumination. Everything is painted at its maximum surface quality: the silk cloth has a sheen, the pewter plate reflects the room, the lemon peel is waxy and curling. The moral: look at how beautiful material things are, knowing they will not last.

refs.name: Steins;Gate — Research Lab Conspiracy
refs.use: Time travel thriller, Akihabara otaku world, conspiracy wall, Okabe Rintaro drama
refs.dna: White Fox's Steins;Gate grammar: the Future Gadget Lab as a paranoid cathedral. The conspiracy wall behind Okabe — red thread connecting newspaper clippings, message printouts, photographs of historical events — the physical evidence of a theory too large for any mind to hold at once. The Akihabara environment: maid café signs, anime merchandise, the specific ambient noise of electric town. The 1:1:1 color relationship: the amber of CRT monitor light, the red of D-Mail error messages, the clinical white of SERN's underground. Okabe's chuunibyou laugh, his trench coat, his phone permanently at his ear. The visual grammar of a man who knows too much and cannot share it.

refs.name: Batman Animated — Timm Art Deco Noir
refs.use: Noir detective, city crime drama, shadow staging, retro cartoon, hero-villain moral tension
refs.avoid: photorealism, modern anime style, over-detailed environments
refs.dna: German Expressionist-meets-Art Deco city geometry, extreme value simplification (solid flat black shadow fills, flat gray mid-tones, white accent), angular exaggerated architecture against purple-blue night sky, single amber or white light cone isolating subject from geometric black shadow mass, bold graphic design sensibility every frame a design poster

refs.name: Code Geass — Strategic Chess Visual
refs.use: Chess strategic drama, mecha Britannia, Lelouch tactical genius, CLAMP character design
refs.avoid: Geass eye symbol, exact character designs, action battle sequences
refs.dna: Sunrise's Code Geass grammar: the chessboard as metaphor made literal. Lelouch in the playing position — one piece held between two fingers, the opponent's position already resolved, the endgame visible only to him. The CLAMP character design: impossibly long limbs, ornate school uniforms, the Geass eye rendering (one eye glowing with fractal-petal red pattern). Knightmare Frame mechs: the Sutherland in Britannian purple-gold, the Lancelot in white. The throne room: Britannian empire aesthetic, European Gothic meeting Japanese anime. The visual grammar of one man playing chess against a world that doesn't know the game has already started.

refs.name: Renaissance — Leonardo Sfumato
refs.use: Leonardo sfumato technique, atmospheric haze, soft-focus transition, Italian Renaissance mastery
refs.avoid: photorealism, modern color grade, busy digital texture, harsh lighting
refs.dna: Leonardo da Vinci's sfumato technique: the edge of every form dissolves into the next, as if everything is seen through a fine atmospheric haze — the smoke-effect (sfumato means 'smoked' in Italian) that gives the faces of the Madonna and Saint Anne their uncanny quality. The boundary between the figure and the background is never sharp — it is a graduated transition of tone, the form emerging from the shadow rather than being outlined against the light. The mathematical perspective is invisible because the haze makes exact edges impossible. The palette: warm amber earth tones, the specific sfumato grey-blue of the background mountains, skin that has been glazed many times to build depth. The human face as Leonardo's most solved problem.

refs.name: Soviet Constructivism — Diagonal Power
refs.use: Soviet Constructivist design, Rodchenko grammar, diagonal energy, political graphic power
refs.avoid: specific political iconography, realistic portraiture, organic decoration
refs.dna: Alexander Rodchenko and El Lissitzky's Constructivist grammar: the diagonal as the energy principle. Where the bourgeois poster places the figure in calm horizontals, the Constructivist poster tilts everything 30-45 degrees — the worker's arm, the factory tower, the beam of the searchlight. The photomontage: real photographic elements combined in impossible compositions, the Lenin face at 5 meters scale above a crowd. Three colors only: red, black, white — the proletarian palette. Typography as architecture: the letters are structures, the word-block is a building. The visual argument: progress has a direction, and it is diagonal, upward, and to the future.

refs.name: Medieval Illuminated Manuscript
refs.use: Medieval manuscript illumination, gold leaf, lapis blue, Gothic script, devotional grammar
refs.avoid: photorealism, perspective depth, secular modern staging, digital clean finish
refs.dna: Medieval illuminated manuscript grammar (Paris, ca. 1250-1400): the folio as sacred object. The margin illumination: pen-and-ink naturalistic detail (birds, insects, hybrid creatures — drolleries) that frames the divine text without touching it. The historiated initial: the first letter of a psalm or Gospel passage contains within its form a scene from the narrative it introduces, painted in egg tempera at thumbnail scale. The palette: ultramarine blue from lapis lazuli (more expensive than gold by weight), vermillion, burnt sienna, lead white, gold leaf applied first and burnished. The visual rule: the gold must be present, because this is not paint — it is a portal to the divine, and the divine is luminous.

refs.name: Turkish Folk Art — Iznik and Anatolian
refs.use: Iznik tile art, Ottoman ceramic grammar, cobalt arabesques, tulip motif, tile pattern
refs.avoid: Western realism, photorealism, non-geometric organic forms
refs.dna: İznik tile grammar (16th century Suleiman era): the cobalt-blue ground as the sky, the arabesques as the geometry of paradise. Carnation and tulip motifs rendered in cobalt, manganese purple, and a specific bright red called 'İznik red' (actually a slip raised in relief that caught the light differently). The vine arabesque extends continuously without terminus, repeating through geometric progression. The white ground becomes blue becomes pattern becomes white again in infinite recursion. Ottoman imperial tiles: the Süleymaniye and Blue Mosque interiors are the standard. The visual argument: the pattern is not decoration but cosmology — the arabesque is the visual grammar of the infinite.

refs.name: Soul Warmth
refs.use: Emotional warmth, jazz club intimacy, quiet human moments, urban tenderness, grief and joy
refs.dna: Pete Docter's Soul: amber jazz-club practicals at 3am, the piano bench and the quiet of New York before the city wakes. Skin rendered with subsurface warmth, faces modeled for emotional specificity — the slight asymmetry that makes a character real, not beautiful. Upper-frame negative space breathes. Palette: warm amber, dusty gold, soft periwinkle, the particular deep blue of NYC night. Emotional truth through restraint — the quiet held moment, not the big gesture. Tears come from the smallest things: the right chord, an empty chair, light through venetian blinds.

refs.name: Apple Commercial Grammar
refs.use: Product reveal, premium hardware, material craft, engineering excellence, minimalist brand storytelling
refs.dna: Apple's product theology (Jony Ive/Lee Clow era): a single device in absolute void — the background not white but the specific near-white that reads as light itself. One edge-light from above-left grazes the aluminum chassis, defining object geometry with jeweler's precision. The glass surface holds one perfect specular highlight traveling the curved surface. No shadow is accidental — each is designed. The frame holds near-silence. The product is not demonstrated; it is worshipped. The visual promise: the person who uses this object is the kind of person who notices what most people miss.

refs.name: Nike Energy
refs.use: Athletic achievement, motivational energy, physical peak, sport action, aspirational heroism
refs.dna: Wieden+Kennedy's Nike grammar: an athlete frozen at the single 1/1000th-second frame of maximum biomechanical commitment — highest point of a jump, full sprint extension, peak pivot torque — the frame that contains all the training that preceded it. Hard directional key from one side creates a motion-implying shadow streak across the ground. The background is irrelevant; the body is everything. Palette: darkness with one energy accent — Nike orange flash, electric green, court red. The silhouette reads as a logo. No corporate framing — pure athletic will, raw and singular.

refs.name: DreamWorks — Hero Comedy Grammar
refs.use: Adventure comedy, expressive heroism, buddy dynamics, epic world with comedic protagonist
refs.dna: DreamWorks adventure grammar (How to Train Your Dragon/Kung Fu Panda era): the hero in a pose exactly halfway between genuine courage and total self-satisfaction — one beat past confident, just tipping toward comedic. Warm adventure key from above-angle, stylized caricature proportions (big expressive hands, arched brow, medium-large eyes that can hold both heroism and panic). Ensemble comedy staging: side characters in reaction, reinforcing the hero's misplaced confidence. The world behind them — mountains, ocean, ancient city — is genuinely epic and they are charmingly, gloriously inadequate to it. Palette: warm amber adventure gold, sky cerulean, earthy greens.

refs.name: Illumination — Pop Comedy Grammar
refs.use: High-energy comedy, crowd scenes, simple readable forms, cheerful chaos, family comedy
refs.dna: Illumination's maximum-saturation grammar: primary-color boldness, zero shadow complexity, overhead ambient key that flattens depth into graphic readability. Round forms, big expressive shapes, instantly readable at any scale. The comedy lives in the gap between a character's total confidence in their terrible plan and the physics about to destroy it. Clean background zones, one bold focal accent per scene, chaotic but joyful crowd staging. The emotional register is cheerful chaos — not ironic, not melancholy, genuinely full of combustible fun. The perfect pop illustration brought to life.

refs.name: Digimon — Digital Adventure Grammar
refs.use: Digital world texture, child adventure, creature partnership, wonder, crossing into another world
refs.dna: Toei's Digimon grammar (1999 season): the threshold between the real world and the Digital World — a glowing grid horizon, pixel-break texturing at object edges where digital physics begin to show, the warm orange of adventure-anime sunset sky behind digital terrain. Human child framed with partner creature, establishing scale (creature larger but protective). Bold cel-shading, clean outlines, optimistic palette: deep blue digital sky, warm orange adventure light, primary color boldness on both child and creature. The frame's promise: there is another world, and you belong in it.

refs.name: Football Stadium Energy Grammar
refs.use: Stadium scale, crowd atmosphere, broadcast sport, national pride, live event energy
refs.dna: The grammar of broadcast football: stadium corner shot — 70,000 humans as a color field, the green pitch a geometric precision in floodlight white below. Four flood-column beams rise into the night sky. Wide 24mm from just behind the goal line, the pitch converging toward midfield in perspective that emphasizes ritual scale. The athlete on the pitch is a tiny figure in a cathedral — sport as ancient theater. National team color palette in the crowd mass. The camera is absolutely still: this is not chaos, this is ceremony. The frame holds the collective breath of 70,000 people in absolute silence before the action begins.

refs.name: Arcane Zaun Dark
refs.use: Dark drama, undercity atmosphere, industrial gothic, corruption, mature storytelling
refs.avoid: Copying Vi/Jinx/Silco likenesses, actual League of Legends lore
refs.dna: Fortiche Studio's Zaun grammar: oil-paint brush strokes visible in the texture of every surface — painted realism, the opposite of photographic. The palette is poison: toxic green-yellow atmospheric haze from chem-tech processing, ember-orange warmth from chimney practicals, deep shadow masses consuming 70% of the frame. Architecture is industrial gothic — exposed pipes, industrial weight of corroded iron and copper, grated walkways, chemical steam rising. There is no overhead source here — illumination rises from below: glowing chemical pipes, ember fire, bioluminescent drainage. No surface is clean. The beauty is inseparable from the corruption.

refs.name: Spider-Verse Gwen Watercolor
refs.use: Creativity, music, self-expression, art, softer emotional content, dance, performance
refs.avoid: Gwen Stacy likeness, Spider-Woman costume, Marvel IP
refs.dna: Spider-Verse's Ghost-Spider visual grammar: watercolor wash fields bleeding at their edges, the background dissolving into soft pink-teal-white washes as if the scene is occurring inside a living illustration. Clean ink outlines hold the forms while the world behind them is pure emotion made visible. Slight chromatic offset registration — deliberate misalignment as aesthetic choice. Pastel radial spotlight on the subject, flat pop-art fields of color, negative space used freely. The visual logic: this is an image of a feeling, not a photograph of a place. Music, creativity, light movement, states of grace — things that can't be photographed, only painted.

refs.name: MHA Hero Panel Grammar
refs.use: Courage, heroism, effort, achievement, sports climax, decisive moment, peak determination
refs.avoid: Specific hero costumes (All Might, Deku, etc.), Quirk names, UA High School
refs.dna: My Hero Academia's shounen heroism grammar: low-angle up-shot against an open sky or light-blasted background, bold black-white speed lines radiating from the peak-effort point, the body at maximum extension, the expression at maximum determination. Cel-shading with strong rim light, clean linework at high contrast, comic-panel composition logic — tight crop, single decisive figure, the world beyond them a blur of motion or energy. Palette: blue sky, white power-release flash, warm amber hero-color accent. The visual declaration: all might is earned, nothing is given, and everything is possible if you give everything you have.

agents.name: Idea Generator
agents.summary: Chooses Production Path before scenario. Turns weak client briefs into routes, recommendation, scene architecture and MAMILAS handoff.

agents.name: Master Brief
agents.summary: Creates strict path-aware Final Brief, resolves path conflicts, protects Render Lock, source integrity, scene IDs and @tags.

agents.name: Image Director
agents.summary: Reads Production Path before World. Real path = photoreal/commercial/live-action frames. Animation path = tactile metaphor. Preserves @tags/logo/text.

agents.name: Motion Director
agents.summary: Pure motion only. Reads Production Path before camera. Real path = restrained filmic. Animation = material physics. 5-6s beat/split aware.

agents.name: Proof Director
agents.summary: Fails contamination, skipped IDs, weak render locks, text/logo risk, scene source loss, generic AI-video smell.

agents.name: Suno Music
agents.summary: Path-aware instrumental score, Custom Mode thinking, no vocals unless requested, VO-safe mix.

agents.name: Client Pitch
agents.summary: Turns Final Brief into client-facing concept rationale and approval text without exposing prompt mechanics.

golden.name: IMAGE · Product Hero · Nano Banana
golden.bad: A cinematic shot of a premium phone case, dramatic lighting, ultra realistic, 4K.
golden.why_bad: Generic. No geometry lock, logo/text rule, material behavior, lens, reflection, surface fidelity, start-frame stability.
golden.gold: Ultra-real premium commercial studio frame, exact product geometry locked, macro 100mm lens, clean black negative space, controlled gold rim light across aramid fiber texture, crisp edge highlights, realistic micro-scratches and weave depth, stable logo/packaging plane if present, shallow depth of field, product centered as hero object, no CGI plastic sheen, no warped text, no extra objects, frozen cinematic start frame ready for motion.

golden.name: IMAGE · Human Testimonial · Realism
golden.bad: A happy customer talking to camera in a nice office, cinematic.
golden.why_bad: Stock testimonial. No skin texture, eye-line, wardrobe fabric, practical light, human imperfection, location credibility.
golden.gold: Photoreal human portrait film frame, 50mm lens at eye height, natural skin texture with subtle pores and asymmetry, believable eye-line slightly off-camera, real wardrobe fabric with visible weave, soft practical key light from window left, muted office background with authentic depth, restrained color grade, human-scale framing, calm expression, no plastic skin, no fake smile, no beauty smoothing, frozen start frame.

golden.name: IMAGE · Education · Object-led
golden.bad: A Pixar classroom where kids learn about liquid measurements.
golden.why_bad: Too literal/classroom. No tactile metaphor, dominant lesson-object, motion hook.
golden.gold: Premium 3D Pixar-quality animated feature frame inside a handcrafted soft-clay learning world, one oversized transparent measuring beaker as the dominant lesson-object, warm classroom sunlight, tactile rounded materials, measurement marks physically engraved on a wooden base, liquid surface paused halfway with a visible light reflection ready to move, foreground clay droplets leading the eye, clean readable composition, no flat worksheet, no random labels, motion-ready start frame.

golden.name: MOTION · Product Hero · Kling 3.0
golden.bad: Camera zooms in while light sweeps over the phone case.
golden.why_bad: Lazy zoom. No parallax, no physical reflection choreography, no start/end discipline, no tail hold.
golden.gold: Low macro dolly slides once along the black tabletop, a blurred foreground edge giving parallax; as it arrives a single gold rim highlight travels once across the aramid weave and lands on the logo plane, which stays fixed and readable. Product geometry locked, no shape change. The move completes by ~5s into a three-quarter hero hold with clean negative space and a faint specular shimmer. Negative field: morphing, warping logo/text, changing product shape, extra objects, flicker. For more coverage add a second short beat instead of stretching this one.

golden.name: MOTION · Animation Education · Kling 3.0
golden.bad: The camera slowly pushes in and the objects glow.
golden.why_bad: Generic AI-video. No material/world physics, no educational reveal, no environment reaction, no final frame logic.
golden.gold: Child-eye push toward the clay lesson table; the liquid surface rises in ONE clean physical move and stops exactly at the engraved mark, a single warm highlight settling on the glass. Beaker geometry, table, engraved text and the characters stay fixed; one clay droplet wobbles once from the motion. Main event done by ~5s, then a stable three-quarter final hold with the level locked at the mark. Negative field: morphing, warping engraved text, changing faces, opening mouths, extra liquid, flicker. This beat completes well within Kling 3.0's 10s window — for more VO coverage, chain a second approved beat rather than stretching this one.

golden.name: MOTION · Documentary Civic · Kling 3.0
golden.bad: Drone shot of the city with cinematic music and people smiling.
golden.why_bad: Stock civic montage. No human scale, no motivated camera, no authentic public texture.
golden.gold: Handheld micro-drift begins at child height beside a real sidewalk, following the chalk line as pedestrians pass naturally in soft background parallax. The camera does not fly; it moves like a documentary operator walking slowly, letting practical morning light reveal the civic texture of paving stones, storefront glass and passing shadows. by the late beat the line leads into a modest public square where children’s movement creates the reveal. Final hold: stable human-scale frame, no politician hero shot, no stock crowd, no artificial glow.

golden.name: SUNO · Documentary / Corporate · v5.5
golden.bad: Epic inspiring cinematic music with emotional vocals.
golden.why_bad: Too trailer-like and vocals fight voice-over.
golden.gold: Narration-safe instrumental documentary score, 82 BPM, soft felt piano pulse, restrained warm strings, low sub texture, subtle brushed percussion, no vocals, no lead melody during dialogue, gentle rise at midpoint, final warm resolve, clean mix with voice-over space, custom mode style layers: documentary, civic warmth, restrained cinematic, human scale.

golden.name: SUNO · Education · v5.5
golden.bad: Happy fun cheerful uplifting kids learning music.
golden.why_bad: Mood-filler. No named instruments, no bpm/groove, no VO pocket, no scene-mapped arc — Suno drifts.
golden.gold: Narration-safe instrumental education motif, 96 BPM light swing, felted celesta lead with a marimba answer, pizzicato string pulse, one soft clarinet, gentle shaker (no kick clipping the VO); clear midrange pocket left open for the narration VO and ducked under dialogue; intro one curious celesta phrase, build adds marimba and pizzicato at the concept reveal, one small peak on the transformation beat, warm resolve on a single held celesta note. Custom mode style layers: children's education, warm, curious, tactile, hummable. Exclude: vocals, lyrics, big drums, EDM drops, trailer brass, busy percussion.

golden.name: SUNO · Product Hero · v5.5
golden.bad: Luxury epic premium powerful emotional product music.
golden.why_bad: Trailer cliché. No instruments, no bpm, no space, no designed reveal sound, fights the VO.
golden.gold: Narration-safe premium product bed, 84 BPM, muted felt-piano pulse over a low sub-bass foundation, one designed signature synth tone reserved only for the product reveal, wide clean room; midrange pocket kept open for VO and ducked under dialogue; intro sparse pulse, build adds the sub at the macro pass, a single signature hit lands on the logo/reveal beat, clean resolve on one sustained low note. Custom mode style layers: premium commercial, restrained, confident, modern, spacious. Exclude: vocals, busy percussion clipping the VO, trailer brass, genre drift.

golden.name: PROOF · Reference IP Safety
golden.bad: Make the character look like Ichigo holding a Zanpakuto in Soul Society uniform.
golden.gold: FAIL. Reference contamination. Convert to: high-contrast black/orange blade-pressure grammar, negative-space slash timing, spiritual-energy abstraction, original character design, no Ichigo likeness, no Zanpakutō design, no uniform/logo/world copy.

golden.name: IMAGE · Game Art Direction · Zelda/Elden Mix
golden.bad: A Zelda and Elden Ring inspired fantasy world with a hero.
golden.why_bad: Names references but no translation; risks IP copy.
golden.gold: Original open-air mythic adventure frame: soft cel-shaded green-gold landscape meets decayed gothic ruin scale, low sun cutting through fogged stone arches, tiny original child explorer silhouette for scale, ancient-tech glow embedded in broken masonry, wide 24mm lens, clear foreground grass motion hook, no Link/Tarnished likeness, no Triforce, no armor/logo/weapon copy, IP-safe synthesis of exploration mood and mythic ruin gravity.

regression.name: Real Path Contamination
regression.input: Ultra Real Commercial + clay diorama + Pixar render lock
regression.expected: FAIL: real path cannot inherit clay/Pixar/diorama unless hybrid override is active.

regression.name: Source Loss
regression.input: Raw source 1200 chars, scene source 980 chars
regression.expected: FAIL: coverage below 100%; return to Lossless Ingest.

regression.name: Logo/Text Morph Risk
regression.input: Product hero with logo visible + motion prompt moves surface aggressively
regression.expected: FIX: freeze logo/text plane; only camera/light/reflection moves.

regression.name: Lazy Motion
regression.input: Camera slowly zooms in, lights glow, cinematic
regression.expected: FIX: add motivated camera arc, physical action, environment reaction, final tail hold.

regression.name: IP Reference Misuse
regression.input: Make it look like Luffy on the Thousand Sunny
regression.expected: FAIL/FIX: abstract into sunny ocean adventure, ship-deck scale, bold primaries; no Luffy/Sunny copy.

regression.name: Concept Monotony
regression.input: Brief with many CONCEPT lines but few unique subjects
regression.expected: FIX: too many scenes reuse the same concept; group source into thematic beats or deepen the concept bank.

regression.name: Fallback Leak
regression.input: Brief where multiple scenes use generic fallback concept templates
regression.expected: FAIL: generic fallback concepts repeated; expand EDU_SOURCE_BANK patterns or re-ingest source.

regression.name: Generic Bridge Subject
regression.input: Scene where primary subject is a generic placeholder instead of a concrete visual object
regression.expected: FIX: primary subject is a generic bridge phrase; replace with a concrete visible object from the source.
